Guacamelee is a really fun and fast-paced metroidvania set in Mexico, and it is loosely based on Mexican mythology and culture. The game doesn't take itself particularly seriously, there's plenty of jokes and silly situations, and there also many "side quest" that are actually parodies of other games/genres, and I often found the punchlines at the end of each pretty funny.  The combat is fun, but where the game really shines is the platforming.

Gameplay is decent and humor is similar. If you enjoyed the first game, you would enjoy this. It has completely lost the weight behind the story that the original had, but that might just be nostalgia speaking.

整体都还不错，就是刚进游戏前期卖相有点差，不知道的还以为是什么喜加一游戏 难度上属于循序渐进的（温水煮青蛙）越往后操作越复杂，还有5个超难隐藏关，好在只通主线完全不需要管这些 需要注意的是，游戏虽然定位是类银，但流程上几乎是线性的，基本属于获得一个新能力-打一个地图-再获得一个新能力 这样的循环，很少需要走回头路（不算优点也不算缺点） 画面上有个小缺点：最高仅支持2k分辨率，不支持4k 个人游戏评分：90/100 个人总评分：89/100

I've played Guacamelee, and this game has all the same issues I had with that one. The platforming is frustrating and the combat is not fun. Same lame referential humor as in the first game. I like metroidvanias and I don't mind a harder difficulty when it's done right, this ain't it. About the only positive thing I have to say is I like the art style and all the mexican culture stuff is pretty novel.

Esta segunda entrega me pareció una gran secuela y, sinceramente, una saga excelente para empezar en el género metroidvania. No castiga demasiado al jugador, su progreso se siente natural y el diseño de niveles es bastante intuitivo, así que explorar y orientarte nunca se vuelve frustrante.  Mantiene ese estilo visual colorido inspirado en la cultura mexicana que le da mucha personalidad. Las habilidades están muy bien integradas y hacen que avanzar y descubrir secretos sea bastante satisfactorio.  Si buscas entrar al género sin sentirte abrumado, esta saga es una muy buena puerta de entrada. Divertida, accesible y con el suficiente reto como para engancharte sin castigarte de más.

AVISO NAO É METROIDVANIA, PARECE QUE É MAS NÃO É..... ELE É TOTOALMENTE LINEAR LITERALMENTE, não existe isso de "explorar" vc só anda reto seguindo pra onde tem que ir e já era... bom pra jogar com amigo.

Faccio questa recensione a quasi dal termine del gioco perché ho già tutto il materiale che mi occorre per esprimere il mio giudizio finale sull'opera. Come sentito da altri utenti e, su questo, mi appoggio a loro, Guacamelee! 2 prende ciò che di buono ed interessante aveva il 1 portandolo ad un livello superiore. Più a fuoco, più godibile, più fluido e meglio lavorato a livello artistico e videoludico. Ha saputo sfoltire varie cose che nel primo erano un pò grezze e rudimentali e qui l'hanno reso un gioco interessante e appagante. La durata aumenta anche un pò di più, il ché è un bene. Ma i problemi purtroppo restano... Anzitutto è rimasta la fastidiosissima mappa ad intrecci, con pochissimi teletrasporti e tanta confusione nel capire dove andare o meno per raggiungere X tappa. Ma non è tanto quello il punto, quanto il fatto che, quando dovrai tornare indietro a completare le mappe ti devi FARE OBBLIGATORIAMENTE tutti i platforming e sezioni di stage lunghe e stralunghe perché hanno piazzato 1 singolo teletrasporto per area, rendendo la meccanica pallosa come nel 1. Tant'è che io mollai il 100% perché non mi stimolava proprio investire decine e decine di minuti per ogni zona da esplorare. Qui è ancora peggio, perché, seppur il gioco aumenti sensibilmente la difficoltà, tocca dei picchi di frustrazione piuttosto ingenerosi non dandoti nemmeno i checkpoint per fare un pò di progresso per volta. Un vero peccato, perché la sfida è alta e fatta abbastanza bene ma l'esecuzione lascia a desiderare. Ci sono alcune sfide dove sembra che, gli sviluppatori, non hanno minimamente bilanciato il timing con ciò che c'è da risolvere nella mappa. Andando quasi all'estremo del perfect pixel per risolverlo e superarlo. Ed alcune sezioni in cui il platforming dura oltre 30 secondi di esecuzione senza nemmeno un checkpoint, tremendamente try & error. Fanno sempre il solito errore molti giochi, per alzare la difficoltà devono aggiungere per forza i mancati checkpoint o non bilanciare delle sezioni, trasformando una sfida godibile in un qualcosa di frustrante e sbagliato. Perché la difficoltà in sé è molto interessante, sa essere difficile e meritocratica per chi ha un alto skill check ma, in alcuni punti, ti tocca avere resistenza muscolare assorbendoti decine e decine di morti ingiuste perché alcune sezioni sono poco bilanciate o troppo lunghe. Ma vah beh, evito il 100% per il primo motivo citato. Il secondo onestamente mi cambia il giusto. Per il resto il gioco è ben riuscito e lo consiglio ugualmente, ma a prezzo pieno meh.. non proprio. Soprattutto perché per quanto divertente e ben focussato, ti dà un senso di antipatia allucinante. A volte mi stava proprio sulle scatole fare la qualunque, non era incazzatura. Era pura antipatia che pochi giochi mi hanno suscitato. Perché aveva un potenziale per diventare un giocone e s'è perso in un bicchier d'acqua, soprattutto perché i comandi spesso so ballerini, il personaggio si attanaglia e appiccica alle pareti senza che gli viene indicato di farlo solo perché sei vicino alla sporgenza e non c'è una totale libertà di controllo ma il tutto decade in legnosità e macchinosità, e infine spesso le fight diventano troppo caotiche e stupide e le skill e la strategia se né vanno un pò a farsi benedire portandoti a spammare super mosse come se non ci fosse un domani a testa bassa. Ripeto, complessivamente è un bel gioco ma potevano fare MOLTO di più sia sulla difficoltà non calibrata perfettamente, sia dal punto di vista dei comandi e dei combattimenti, lasciato troppo acerbo e poco mirato. Di certo rispetto al 1 è un buonissimo passo in avanti, mi ha divertito di più e questo è già un qualcosa. Se dovessero farne un terzo, spero che questi difetti vengono appiattiti senza toccare la struttura perché è solida e vincente secondo me.

A fantastic sequel and another must-play for Metroidvania fans. The combat feels even better here — flashy, satisfying, and really fun. The world is well-designed, the art style is even more beautiful than in the first game, and the humor and atmosphere are excellent. Once again, there are plenty of secrets and easter eggs. A very polished game that takes around 30 hours to fully complete. Highly recommended.

Guacamelee! 2 é um aprimoramento do seu antecessor em todos os aspectos.  Seja na sua mecânica, isto é, um beat'em up metroidvania - que dessa vez focou MESMO no beat'em up e na sua dificuldade - seja no seu lúdico e debochado humor, ou na sua arte.  É um jogo que apesar das referências e dos memes, ele vai muito além disso, fazendo sátiras até de coisas como o MCU / DCU [o "mexiverso"], não deixando de homenagear outros jogos clássicos e até mesmo bem, os haters do primeiro jogo!  A minha única crítica é que eles pesaram mesmo a mão na dificuldade, inclusive fazendo piada da dificuldade do 1º jogo BEM NO COMEÇO DO 2º e mantendo aquele nível já de cara - e piorando gradualmente a cada minuto que passa. Por isso, é um jogo bem nichado, não acho que irá agradar qualquer um  No mais, outro jogasso e infelizmente o último da franquia até então

Не блещет сюжетом, но геймплейная составляющая стоит того, чтобы в неё играть. Относиться серьёзно к происходящему - портить впечатление от игры. Если есть опыт в платформерах (проходили полностью) - советую включить тяжёлую сложность перед первым прохождением (гайды найдёте в сообществе как это сделать). Игра умеренно сложная (до ебучей сложности нехватает того, чтобы боссы были сильнее, тк обычные боссы достаточно просты, а финального и одного из необязательного испытания можно зачизить), если не исследовать все локации, но становится сильно сложнее, когда вы исследуете все локации и пытаетесь собрать куриный ключ для хорошей концовки (конвеерные ленты в затопленной лавой кишке будут вам очень рады). На прохождение 100% без гайдов у меня ушло 14 часов и приличное количество смертей. У вас может уйти больше, если вы не играли в первую часть столько сколько я.

又是一款神作类银，时常设计的太好了，不长也不短，上班族也能通关。 战斗方面，手感很好，难度适中，刚好有点难的程度，基本不会卡关。 跳跳乐方面不打全收集不算太难，难点全集中在完美结局的收集上了，比丝之歌难多了，有个别有蔚蓝B面难度。 全收集不看攻略也能找齐，比一代强，好评。 音乐也很不错。 总体来说爱玩类银的可以无脑入，非常推荐。
二代的墨西哥卷饼味依旧浓厚，众多老山羊NPC的人物对话也是超有笑料，人物技能上多了很多小鸡形态的能力，鸡的存在感更强了，更别说还有小鸡光明会，里面的鸡叫背景音乐非常魔性，并且二代的跳跳乐挑战难度只增不减，平台跳跃爱好者狂喜

Guacamelee is a great metroidvania! The music is great; I would bob my head to the music as I chose to select what skill to level up, or as I made a decision on what part of the map to explore next.  The story is very light and comedic: for instance, you can turn into a chicken. Every now and then, there is also a very direct reference to an internet meme, indie game, etc. -- there is a even a joke about gacha games which got a good chuckle from me. But like any great comedy, there is a subtle tone of heart or seriousness in the joke or storybeat, like Salvador's backstory. While the narrative is relatively barebones, there is a theme about being content and thankful for what you have, and at the same time, there is a theme about regret.  Of course, the centerpiece of this metroidvania is its gameplay. Guacamelee is a challenging game -- even just aiming for the normal difficulty and ending. Some parts of the story are harder than the side content, and a decent amount of that side content is very hard on its. The story will challenge you to understand the gameplay mechanics and the platforming at a deep level, and the side content will force you to master both. My main gripe would be that the game is not very consistent with keyboard input: sometimes, if I enter multiple inputs consecutively, sometimes the input would buffer or cancel that next one, and the game requires that you be very fast in your reactions, so it gets very frustrating when you expect your luchador to perform a certain action but he/she doesn't. For instance, when I jump down from a platform, sometimes the jump would perform twice -- effectively canceling my movement as I jump back onto platform. My other gripe is that certain elements on screen can be ambiguous if they are part of the foreground or background, so it may get confusing where to go next, what is main story content or side story content, etc. I also wish I could see more of the level since part of the platforming requires understanding what is the next segment of the level, and if you fail to certain elements in the level, you can retry instantly but if you fail at others, you have to instantly restart from the beginning of the checkpoint.

GREAT sequel to the first game. Everything here is basically the first game turned up to 11.  The normal-mode story is pretty easy, and the bosses could’ve been harder, but that’s what hard mode is for, I guess. (I haven’t gotten around to playing hard mode yet at the time of writing this review, just assuming based on how the first game handled it.)  All of the maps are INSANELY huge. Take the biggest map from the first game and add like 50% more area, and that’s basically every zone in this game. There’s a ton to explore, and honestly I’m all for it. The game is SO gorgeous.  The humor is great too. I might just have a really dumb sense of humor, but the self-aware jokes and the game occasionally mocking the player are right in my lane.  Some of the optional challenges are insanely difficult, but I don’t see that as a problem. They’re optional, if you can’t handle them, just skip them. Personally, I enjoy struggling through something 20–30 times before finally beating it. The delayed reward makes it feel so much better.  And yes, I’m specifically talking about the Crucible chicken parkour room required for the good ending. Angriest hour of my life… but also one of the happiest moments when Juan finally gets back to his family. :D  The story is great. I absolutely LOVE all the characters, and I’m really glad they went full fan service by bringing characters from the first game back. They kinda bastardized my beautiful king Jaguar Javier, but I can forgive it since it’s not the real Jaguar Javier from the good timeline, so whatever.  I found myself both raging and laughing my ass off within the span of a few hours.  I definitely recommend playing the first game first, if you like it, you’ll almost certainly love this one too.  11/10 - HERO OF THE MEXIVERSE!!!

This is a decent sequel carrying over all of the abilities of the first game with a few new ones added.  If you liked the first one, then you'll like this one as the music and combat stand out as strong selling points on par with the first game.  Though in my honest opinion, I thought the first game was paced and balanced better.  While playing, I felt like there might have been some sort of controller lag eating the inputs as every now and then I found myself suddenly unable to turn the character around or button presses not matching up to the number of hits the character throws out.   Also, one of the changes from the first game that I thought was a terrible idea was turning a certain enemy type that counts down until it does a screen-wide attack from just doing damage and a knockdown in the first game into an instant-death in this game if you don't get it in time.    Overall, I thought this was just a fun game all around.

Just like the second [b]Ori[/b] game, I found myself finally checking the sequel of a game I played in the mid-2010s, only many years after its release. And I felt at home immediately: just like back then, the first upgrade is an uppercut move that also functions as a further jump, as an effective double jump is found only much later.  Truth be told, it could be argued (also in general) that [b]Guacamelee 2[/b] plays a bit too safe, at least based on my memories of the original, with even the sequence of the main upgrades to be found being more or less the same, the Eagle Boost being the only new maneuver for Juan in his regular form. On the other hand, with such a solid framework from the first game, there was not really much that needed to be fixed, not when the controls are still so responsive and pulling off combos in fights and sequences of moves in platforming challenges is so effortless.  The chicken form gets more love and becomes the co-star, with even dedicated temples where human form is not allowed, and many passages where its small size and specific abilities are required. There's something incredibly fun about totaling foes as a dive-bombing chicken that can also grab and piledrive them.  The other upgrades that cost money, along with listed conditions to unlock the purchases, can feel intimidating at first, as five whole groups of them are revealed; however, as the fights and challenges ramp up, by the end you will likely have bought most, if not all, of them.  The characteristic colorful and angular visual style is back and better than ever; under the apparent simplicity there is an amazing quantity of detail and, as the ability to switch between two planes of existence eventually returns, it means there are basically two graphical sets for everything, with many elements changing well beyond simply different colors. The soundtrack is an eclectic mix of mariachi-style music and 8-bit sounds. Not sure if a "necessary" change, but cool to listen to. That track related to chicken is the best. Just like the first game, there is a large number of small and big references to other properties, from the opening that clearly parodies the first minutes of [b]Castlevania: Symphony of the Night[/b] (and a funny related achievement), through all those luchadores based on known characters, to... wait, the ugly painting from [b]Katawa Shoujo[/b]?! Memes are relegated to a specific optional area, but the self-awareness about them doesn't make that part less cringey.  Even with generous checkpoints, this is not an easy game (difficulty levels affect the combat but not the platforming gauntlets), and at least in the early part it's better to tackle as many challenges as possible to buy sorely needed upgrades; over the last quarter or so, however, I ended up throwing in the towel on some, including the ones necessary to get the best ending. To my pleasant surprise, however, this time the worse ending is not bad at all; as even completing the "regular" part of the game can get quite challenging - even if, amusingly, I found the second part of the final boss fight easier, like the first was added by the developers only to whittle down the player - one is not "punished" for being a less than perfect player, while the best ones receive an even better version of the ending as a reward.  I've never been a fan of the fights happening often in enclosed spaces you can't move past until you win. It's not like they aren't fun, I sometimes even got pumped when "LUCHA!" appeared on the screen, I just wish the flow of the game wasn't fragmented so much by them. Also, during the times the view is zoomed out (mainly because of huge enemies or sizable swarms), it can be difficult even to make out the player out of the chaos.  Looking back after playing it, however, there's really little to criticize about [b]Guacamelee 2[/b]: it has hair-pulling moments, but for most of the time it's extremely fun, and also funny with its humour and the villains, even if the big bad is of the more sympathetic type; the cutscene detailing his origins is one of the highlights. Over the years the game has been bundled and deeply discounted (80% just recently), really little reason to miss out on a Metroidvania that can stand proud among the bigs of the genre, with its unique flavor of combat that doesn't use the Mexican luchador theme as simple window dressing. With the first episode, it also makes for one of the best duologies of the 2010s (and beyond).

Guacamelee! 2 . A very beautiful and dynamic game! Amazing music, great visual design. I like the combat system and story. . Controls: Left Stick or cross - move Right Stick - dodge A - jump B - special move Y - grab X - attack RT - swap dimension RB - shadow swap LT - dodge LB - pollo Coop Bubble - LB + RB Wall grab - hold Y or move Left Stick Select - map Start - pause . Mechanics and features: Cool short logo; the game starts very fast. Select opens the map, skills, and world map.

Non sapevo cosa aspettarmi e temevo un po' il more of the same, invece, per quanto da una parte lo sia, prende il primo capitolo e lo migliora ulteriormente. Tralasciando la trama che ne fa da contorno, il gioco è coloratissimo, migliora tanto la grafica e le animazioni, andando avanti diventa volutamente e graditamente confusionario (almeno per me che l'ho giocato direttamente in hard), platform e action allo stato puro, caratteristiche molte volte unite dalle zone lotta più "dinamiche". I poteri rimangono gli stessi, ma ci sta poiché già completi, ma a sto giro c'è un piccolo albero delle abilità e viene migliorata la trasformazione "pollo", anch'essa con poteri e importanza anche per storia ed esplorazione. Riguardo quest'ultima, nel primo capitolo ho avuto più la sensazione di ricerca di zone nascoste, mentre qui è come se fossero più "in vista", però il fatto che serve sbloccare i poteri adatti per visitarle non mi ha tolto comunque la voglia di fare backtracking.  Forse un piccolo lato negativo sono le boss fight, per quanto belle sono poche e alcune semplici. Tutto sommato un ottimo secondo capitolo che mi dà speranza di vederne un terzo in futuro.

《墨西哥英雄大混战2》个人评价【 7/10】 【故事剧情  8/10】  在一代的基础上延伸出时间线的设定，有很多搞怪无厘头的时间线  【视觉画面  8/10】  和一代一样，不错的漫画风设计，获取新技能的特写也很搞怪  【音乐音效  7/10】  洗脑的背景音乐，打击音效和小鸡叫声都很魔性  【战斗手感  6/10】  在一代的基上添加了对小鸡的特技，但这也导致他的大份的护盾新增了两种，斜向特技也使得手柄老按错  相比于一代的纯数值提升，二代的技能树系统明显设计的更好，但这也导致抓取反而不吃香了，因为高连击的特技是不消耗技能点的（受伤超过三次才会断掉连击），并且闪避还能取消特技的后摇，因此可以无限使用特技  二代的BOSS都设计的挺好的，但是小怪还是千篇一律，加上后期堆怪严重，而且强制战斗次数很多，导致同质化太严重了，打的很麻木  【探索收集  6/10】  比一代稍好点，不用左跑右跑，大部分收集品勤看地图都能在刚拿到能力去收集，但多了很多即死的陷阱，而且很多跳跳乐都放在主线上了，不做全收集都玩的有点恶心  【攻略收集】  参考价格：steam捆绑包 22.19 （本体+dlc三敌人+dlc试炼之地），不坐全成就可以不买dlc  操作：推荐使用键盘游玩，手柄会同时报废手和手柄  流程：主线剧情最后boss---->收集6钥匙碎片---->真结局---->全地图收集宝箱---->全金牌挑战  参考文献：  全成就：  [PS4/NS《墨西哥英雄大混战2（Guacamelee! 2）》全成就白金攻略 前言](https://psnine.com/topic/34501)  [Steam 社区 :: 指南 :: 墨西哥英雄大乱斗2全成就指南](https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=1792228017)

[h2]I'd probably be less frustrated with this game if they didn't do better first, unfortunately. But comparisons to the first game(s) are as inevitable as they are unflattering. I wish I could give it a mid-rec, but it barely squeaks out into the positives. Just get it on sale.[/h2]  Ultimately, I [i]did[/i] 100% this game, so it was enjoyable enough, but in the future I think I'll just replay the previous games as they were much more fun.  [h3]Pros:[/h3] [list]     [*]Chicken form is significantly more varied and useful, making using it in combat and platforming actually fun. There's also a chicken-specific storyline whose ultimate reward is effectively infinite flight. This skill is both explicitly told to you when you get it, and more thematically appropriate than the previous game's chicken flight.     [*]Outfits are unlocked by finding chests in the world, same as other rewards; and they no longer affect stats, so you can safely wear whatever you like instead of sticking with an unsightly outfit for its benefits. (This isn't true of the DLC outfits, sadly - back to "i hate this skin but it's useful" there, I'm sad to say. However, that's a problem with the DLCs and not the base game.)     [*]Still has beautiful stylization and art, and interesting setpieces.     [*]You hold the directional stick against the wall to grip rather than holding [Grapple]. This is so much better and I see why they went back and added it to STCE.     [*]All of the old moves and abilities are present, along with new ones. No lamenting that you could have done this better with the old moveset, or re-training your muscle memory!     [*]Bosses are easier. You can throw them, they have fewer absolute ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ undodgeable attacks, and the ones they do have are both narrow and telegraphed enough that you can actually get to safety. [/list]  [h3]Meh:[/h3] [list]     [*]The story... exists? It's nothing to write home about, the same type of combo pop culture and video game references and self-referential humor as the last game. If it's your jam, it's well done and mostly doesn't overstay its welcome. If it's not your jam, you're probably going to find it grating. This one's just personal taste!     [*]The story for one of the bosses is just dropped? Where is my full chicken musical (joking)? This isn't needed, it was just slightly weird. Instead we got more time with Jaguar Javier, which is admittedly something more people probably wanted.     [*]Tostada is no longer part of the Move Get art. This isn't really an issue, the art is much more dynamic and Juan is the main character. But I miss it.     [*]The costumes aren't versioned for Juan and Tostada, they each have costumes only for them. Again, since the costumes aren't stats, this is fine but it's another thing I miss. [/list]  [h3]Cons:[/h3] [list]     [*]Controls are significantly worse than the previous game. I went back and played both Guacamelee Gold and STCE to 100% them before this playthru, and it's harder to hit moves, harder to cancel out of them, harder to dodge on a dime, and harder to correct mid-air. I can't tell if it's actually dropping inputs, or if it just has much less tolerance on the direction+button press combos, but it makes combat and platforming feel unfair and clunky once you get any additional moves.     [*]I hate the Eagle Boost. Similar to the previous point, it just doesn't work very well - and the fact that it's required for some of the most challenging platforming in the game is just insult to injury. I failed even easy sections multiple times b/c I grappled instead of boosted, despite being in range of the eagle point and the glowing arrow being present.     [*]Significantly harder platforming and challenges do NOT hold up under the worse controls. Several required sections require tight enough timing that it feels purposefully cruel, and several of the bonus challenges required for 100% (and most of the DLCs) are just RNG whether you'll complete them or not. Add this to...     [*]Significantly less generous checkpointing. You'll be ragequitting as you're sent back to the beginning of a 2-5 [i]minute[/i] long section with combat and platforming. And that's on normal mode! There are also [i]far[/i] mode instant death traps that will send you rooms back, often forcing you to re-get chests you already unlocked b/c you didn't make it back to the entrance safely. These also reload the room and are slower for restarting challenges, leading to additional frustration in the form of repetitive timewasting. There are way more save spots to combat this, but since you often have to go out of your way to remember to hit the save spot it's still a major frustration point that could have been fixed by just having the standard yeet-you-back-to-checkpoint spikes and goo. Remember the Tule Tree mask shard from the first game? Imagine if every time you missed a jump it killed you and set you back to the savepoint at the bottom of the free, before the wall run segment.     [*]Much easier to lose your character in the press of enemies, making combat difficult if not impossible. I think this is a combo of Juan (or various skins) not standing out as well from the background as in the previous game, the zoom being further out, the enemies being physically larger, AND the game spawning too many enemies for the room size. It's possible to lose where you are in a battle with only 4 enemies.     [*]Mean-spirited. Multiple bonus sections are making fun of other games and of the player specifically, in ways that are purposefully not fun to play. This is a more minor point, since technically all of these sections are optional and easy - but you can't skip dialogue or cutscenes, they're time wasters on top of that in terms of gameplay, and you HAVE to go thru these sections to get the best ending. It really feels like the devs should've just made a vent post on their tumblr rather than making their game worse to own people who won't even be playing their game.     [*]This is probably petty, but Juan and Lupita's kids, and the teenager in the one bonus area, look [i]weird.[/i] They're a noticeably different style both from the adults and from other kids, with huge eyes and faces, and their heads are much larger than the adults' heads, and with more looping animation. If all the kids looked like that, it'd probably be fine, it's just jarring when almost every other child is the old style of art. I assume they did that so it's easier to see the kids emote, since they're the whole reason Juan picks the mask back up, but it doesn't really help. [/list]

Guacamelee! 2 is a confident, exuberant sequel from Drinkbox Studios, published by Drinkbox Studios alongside Kepler Ghost, that takes the distinctive identity of the original and pushes it further in nearly every direction. It remains a rare fusion of Metroidvania exploration, combo-driven brawler combat, and demanding platforming, all wrapped in a riotously colorful world inspired by Mexican folklore and pop culture. Rather than reinventing the formula, the sequel refines and expands it, delivering an experience that feels richer, faster, and more self-assured.  The story follows Juan Aguacate several years after his heroic exploits, portraying him as an older, softer version of the legendary luchador who has traded constant danger for family life. This domestic calm is short-lived, as dimensional instability threatens the very fabric of the Mexiverse, pulling Juan back into action. The narrative is deliberately playful, constantly poking fun at sequels, video game tropes, and its own continuity. Fourth-wall breaks, exaggerated character arcs, and self-aware jokes are woven seamlessly into the adventure, ensuring the story remains lighthearted even as the stakes escalate.  Gameplay remains the heart of Guacamelee! 2, and it is where the sequel shines most brightly. Combat is fast, responsive, and deeply satisfying, built around a wide range of melee attacks, throws, and special abilities that can be chained into lengthy combos. Each new ability feels immediately impactful, not only expanding your offensive options but also unlocking new movement and traversal possibilities. The design philosophy ensures that progress is always tied to mastery, encouraging players to internalize mechanics rather than simply unlocking raw power.  Exploration follows a classic Metroidvania structure, with a large interconnected map filled with secrets, optional challenges, and upgrades. Backtracking is frequent but purposeful, as newly acquired abilities reveal clever shortcuts, hidden rooms, and demanding puzzle-platforming sequences. Dimension-shifting mechanics add an extra layer of complexity, requiring players to flip between parallel realities in real time to navigate hazards or defeat enemies. These transitions are fluid and often integrated into high-speed sequences that test both reflexes and spatial awareness.  Platforming difficulty is noticeably more ambitious than in the original game. Late-game sections in particular demand precise timing, quick reactions, and full command of Juan’s moveset. Some sequences border on trial-and-error, especially when platforming is combined with enemy pressure, but the satisfaction of executing these sections cleanly is immense. This increased challenge reinforces the game’s identity as a skill-based experience, though it may feel punishing for players less comfortable with precision platformers.  The return of the chicken form is one of the sequel’s most delightful additions. What was once a novelty is now a fully realized playstyle, complete with its own combat abilities and traversal mechanics. Switching between luchador and chicken forms mid-sequence becomes essential in certain areas, adding variety and humor without ever feeling gimmicky. This expansion of previously minor ideas exemplifies the sequel’s overall approach: take what worked before and give it real depth.  Visually, Guacamelee! 2 is a feast of color and motion. The hand-drawn art style is bold and expressive, with fluid animations that make combat and movement feel alive. Environments burst with personality, from vibrant villages to surreal dimensional rifts, each packed with visual jokes and cultural references. Character designs are exaggerated and memorable, reinforcing the game’s comedic tone while maintaining clarity during fast-paced action. The soundtrack complements this energy perfectly, blending Latin-inspired rhythms with driving rock elements that elevate both combat encounters and exploration.  Local drop-in co-op adds another layer of chaotic fun, allowing up to four players to tackle the adventure together. While cooperative play amplifies the spectacle and humor, it can also heighten the difficulty during platforming-heavy sections, where coordination becomes crucial. Still, the inclusion of co-op enhances replayability and underscores the game’s roots in communal, arcade-style fun.  Despite its strengths, the game occasionally leans heavily on familiarity. Players coming directly from the first Guacamelee! may experience a sense of déjà vu in its structure and pacing, and some optional challenges can feel repetitive in their reliance on strict execution. However, these moments rarely detract from the overall experience, as the game’s enthusiasm and polish consistently carry it forward.  In the end, Guacamelee! 2 stands as a stellar example of how to craft a sequel that respects its origins while confidently expanding its scope. It delivers a vibrant, demanding, and endlessly entertaining adventure that rewards skill, curiosity, and persistence. With its blend of expressive art, inventive mechanics, and joyful self-awareness, it remains one of the most memorable entries in the modern Metroidvania landscape and a clear showcase of Drinkbox Studios’ creative voice.  Rating: 9/10

전작에 비해서 스테이지 수는 적어졌지만, 길이가 길어졌다. 그래서 더 피곤함이 커졌다. 전작에 비해 메트로베니아 요소는 적어졌고 플랫폼 요소는 늘어났다. 거의 1자진행이고 목적지와 다른방향엔 조각이나 돈, 코스튬이 있다. 1에서 겪어봤던, 몬스터색깔놀이와 다른공간 몬스터를 초반부터 넣어놔서 귀찮았다. (후반엔 2패턴이 더 증가함) 닭상태에서 스킬몇개와 갈고리액션이 추가되어 플랫폼 기믹이 늘어났지만, 공간바꾸기, 벽타기, 어퍼컷, 대쉬컷, 뭉개기,염소날기 등등 1에서 사용했던 비슷한 기믹들이 많다. 액션은 거의 그대로 가져왔고 새로운 기술은 없고 임펄소가 사라졌다. 밋밋하게 몬스터들 다 떄려잡아야된다. 몬스터 폭발하는 패시브를 돈주고 배울수 있는데, 별 느낌없다.  2부터 하면 게임스토리라인이 이해가 안갈 것 같고 1부터하면 이건 지겨울 것 같다. -------------------------------- 시련의장까지 후기 7시간 노멀엔딩, 9시간 진엔딩, 11시간 시련의장 정말 스토리 미는데 재미가 없었다.  그나마 열쇠찾는거랑 치킨 일루미나티부분이 조금 재밌기도 했고 가장 발전한게, 시련의장인거 같다. 1에서의 챌린지는 스트레스를 많이 받았지만, 2에선 재밌게 한거같다. 최종적으로 비추천이다.

On a technical level, Guacamelee 2 is a clear improvement over the first game. Combat is smoother, enemy variety is better, boss fights are more interesting, and the soundtrack is excellent. Unfortunately, as an overall experience, I found it deeply flawed—and far less enjoyable than its predecessor.  The biggest issue is the game’s relentless obsession with dialogue and “jokes.” The story is painfully dumb, and if a game forces me to read its writing constantly, I’m going to judge it. Many of the characters are annoying, and most of the humor relies on memes or outside references that feel lazy and cringe. If you enjoy that style of humor, you may get more out of it than I did. For me, it was grating.  Worse, this humor frequently interrupts gameplay. The game regularly forces gimmicky sections on the player: beating up a car, briefly turning into a turn-based RPG, and other novelty moments that exist purely for laughs. I didn’t want to do these things, but the game insists. These interruptions constantly break momentum and made it hard for me to stay engaged.  Which is a shame, because the core gameplay itself is genuinely better. Combat feels improved, enemies are more varied, bosses are generally stronger than in the first game, and the sound design is excellent. I repeatedly found myself wishing the game would just shut up and let me play.  As I put more time into it, two gameplay-related issues increasingly wore me down:  First, gimmicky combat encounters. Some fights feel less about mastering mechanics and more about navigating specific tricks or setups, which undercuts the satisfaction of the beat-em-up combat.  Second, and more importantly, platforming. This game leans extremely hard into difficult platforming challenges. Instant-death traps are everywhere—touch spikes or fall into the void, and you’re immediately reset on the same screen. These sections are frequent, demanding, and unforgiving. If you don’t enjoy precision platforming, I genuinely think this game will be miserable for you.  By the end, while there were moments I enjoyed, I don’t look back on my time with Guacamelee 2 very fondly. I had no desire to pursue the good ending, and that alone makes it hard for me to recommend.  If you enjoy difficult platforming, don’t mind constant dialogue interruptions, and are okay with combat encounters that sometimes feel like gimmicks, you’ll probably have a good time. Personally, I think the first Guacamelee is the better game—it simply lets you play more and interrupts you less.

Volete un consiglio? Conservate il ricordo bellissimo del primo gioco. Questo seguito è un passo indietro sotto ogni punto di vista. La storia è volutamente parodistica, ma rispetto al precedente titolo risulta ancor più esagerata e confusionaria, finendo per perdere qualsiasi tipo di senso o interesse. Neanche a farlo apposta, la "campagna" è più lunga (e credetemi, NON è una cosa positiva). Nemici e scenari sono praticamente ricopiati dal capostipite e non c'è nulla che apparirà ai vostri occhi davvero nuovo. Infine, più di ogni altro aspetto, ritengo i controlli davvero ingiustificabili. La difficoltà delle mappe e degli scontri è basilare, ma l'imprecisione dei comandi rende il tutto artificialmente punitivo. Guacamelee 2, in sostanza, è l'esatto opposto di quello che un'opera skill based dovrebbe essere. Spesso si va avanti per caso, e ancor più spesso si muore per errori nei controlli. Lasciate riposare Juan. E' tempo di andare avanti.

You would think that, seven years after the original game, the developers would create a version that actually has functioning controller inputs in a PRECISION PLATFORMER GAME.  The sheer amount of dropped inputs during a challenging platform sequence, or a chaotic brawl--heck, even when STANDING ON THE GROUND--is staggering. If the controls do not function perfectly when the game demands precision, it is the game that has failed. Far too often I have died due to the game deciding that, yes, I wanted to keep walking to the left into a wall of death spikes, or no, I'd rather not double-jump over a pit, thank you very much.  This is a death-knell of a game of this genre.

As someone who 100-percented the map on a single normal playthrough, I guess I have to mark this game as "recommended" but with some serious caveats.  If you liked the first one, this is just more of the exact same.  Like barely any new abilities, hardly any changes to the formula at all, recycled jokes where they ramped up the cheesiness but not necessarily the actual level of humor.  I guess that can be good or bad depending on how you see it.  This game is also NOT a Metroidvania in the traditional sense.  All of the progression is tightly locked behind "break a colored brick that is blocking your path."  There's no sense of wonder or discovery when you get new powers.  The "locked" areas are marked on your map, so you never have to figure out where to go, it just becomes a checklist of going back to those places that  are CLEARLY marked on your map.  It definitely has elements of Metroidvania's map style, but the progression just doesn't feel the same.  A lot of the screens are just filler with mindless platforming to burn time and make you feel like you are doing something, when in reality it is just artificial gating to slow you down.  There are also a bunch of screens with ambiguous ways to exit where you can technically go off the top or side of the screen, but it just "kills" you and puts you back at the start of the screen.  They don't even damage you or punish you in any way, almost like the devs just throwing in the towel and saying "yeah, we know it looks like there's more to explore, but we didn't actually design anything to put there.  I would say that the harder platforming sections are the best part of this game, especially some of the optional challenges.  Figuring out the patterns to get through some of the screens are really the most satisfying part that kept me playing.  One annoying quirk is that there isn't a uniform scale/zoom for the camera.  Some screens zoom WAY in on you and put you in a cramped little space.  Other screens it zooms WAY out to show you the overall picture of a bigger screen.  Maybe some people won't mind it, but I found it jarring.  The zoomed-in screens are mostly just filler to make you run further when going between two locations.  Content, sure, but not good or interesting content.  In some ways, I finished the game out of spite.  I paid for it, so I'm gonna finish it type of situation.  The game frequently just EATS your inputs if you don't get thumbstick angles absolutely PERFECT.  The controls are not forgiving and will make you angry just as often as they do what you expect.  The combat is repetitive, and there's very little room for your own creativity or skill due to the way the shield system works.  The designers pretty much telegraph what moves you have to use against which enemy, and from there it's mostly just a button-mash till things die.  There's very little real challenge to the combat sans maybe 1 optional area of the game.  But they shoe-horn in SO MUCH forced combat that it starts to becoming annoying by the end.  The number of bosses is staggeringly low, so hopefully you like beating up the same regular enemies over and over.  The progression system is.....(sighhhh) fine.  It does make you more powerful, but by the time I finished I had enough gold to unlock everything in the progression system a full 2nd time.  And there's nothing else to spend your money on, so you just rack up stack on stacks on stacks of absolutely useless currency.  The economy they designed is basically imbalanced in your favor, they just hand you every possible thing by the end, there's no real choice or variety, you'll earn it all before you even get to the end and then there's nothing else to work towards.  If you liked the first one, you will probably like this one (I was lukewarm on the first one too, but got this one for dirt cheap on a Steam sale.)  If you like challenging platforming, you might like this one.  If you're looking for a deep and rewarding combat system and true Metroidvania style exploration, this probably isn't the game for you.

7小时初见通关，我的评价是真是难以评判的游戏，让我加分的地方很多，让我减分的地方也很多。        和一代一样，难度曲线呈上升趋势，前期还会跟你客客气气的，后期大量堆怪和加大难度的跑酷，不过相比一代，我觉得难度下降一大截，并且爽度也有所提高，并且依然设计鬼才，二代相比一代，能力设计增加了小鸡能力，其余还删了一代的无双，代替成变身大鸡鸡了，因此，有很多一代设计的影子，但是因为小鸡能力的出现，设计上有所创新，BOSS设计就相当一般，没有前作那么多意思，跑酷设计相比一代整体下降难度，但是让我印象深刻的跑酷多了很多，设计上出彩的地方不少，总结的话，二代设计整体下降难度，但是依然有很多创新点和全新优秀设计。                接下来我想说一些注意事项。首先，这是一款类银游戏，但是相比其他类银的探索，墨西哥系列的解密要素比其他类银要重的多，几乎是思考大于操作本身，过图和战斗都存在解谜要素和需要动脑子的部分，是一款并非纯粹战斗爽的游戏，当然不是说他战斗不爽，而是我强调的观点是，游戏整体的难度和设计侧重是在解密和观察，战斗系统和解密是高度融合的，这也是为什么我会强调游戏的设计水平，就像解密游戏评判谜题的设计一样，这个游戏应该是这样的评价维度。其次就是老生常谈的难度问题，简单，但是难，这也是为什么我说很多扣分的点，我是空洞细胞双修玩家，细胞全成就，所以我会很适应这个游戏的跑酷和战斗，主角的战斗性能是ACT级别的，但即使这样很多地方我依旧打的想吐，出生的关卡和一些阴间关卡更是让我差点红温，不过还是没有破口大骂。所以，这个我觉得被这个游戏劝退其实是件蛮合理的事的，但是总体难度依旧不高，我个人认为坚持下去是肯定能过的，就要看你是不是那种愿意接受挑战的人了。本作还有很人性化的一点，就是很多难点和阴间关卡是作为宝箱收集的，不作为主线要求，换句话说，打不过可以不打，但是全成就玩家可就有福了。                最后回归游戏本身内容上吧，游戏基调以欢乐幽默为主 ，时间线的设定让游戏充满了乐子，很多有意思的致敬和玩梗，在探索的过程中真心给了我很多惊喜，剧情上更是有笑有泪，我个人认为十分优秀，剧情总体相当套路，但是我还是相当喜欢的。最后的最后，总结一下我是否向你推荐这款游戏，那就是喜欢挑战或者愿意接受有难度的游戏的人，这款游戏是不会让你失望的上等佳作，想要类银质感的玩家同样值得入手体验，而不想有不好体验的自认手残党的就谨慎考虑了。

This game is pretty outstanding as far as sequels go. It takes just about everything from the original, expands upon it, and adds more to look forward to. The writing is humorous, the premise is wacky but with tangible stakes, and the gameplay is satisfying. The combat, which similar to the original, feels fresh due to the new skill tree. There's a lot more chicken shenanigans this time. Instead of being used for simple traversal, Pollo Power is now both fleshed out with a combat system AND is plot relevant. The Chicken Illuminati is always watching... The platforming this time provides even more of a real challenge, even in some of the mandatory sections. I will agree with some of the negative reviews that there is a bit too much lava and few too many "death" as opposed to "teleport-back-to-footing" hazards this time around, but it didn't bother me too much except for when it was intended to. Those Golden Eggs are elusive!  The biggest difference I can say is that the Hard mode this time is ACTUALLY hard. The "Lucha" combat rooms, of which there are many, are packed with higher tier enemies and the platforming is made more challenging in some areas. Those going for 100% completion would benefit from unlocking any challenge achievements or costumes in Normal mode.  Here's hoping we get to return to the Mexiverse in Guacamelee 3!

I absolutely adore this game and I recommend it to anyone who enjoys platformers. It's on the easier side but remains engaging as not to be a snoozefest. I heavily recommend this game. It's worth the money at its normal price but frequently goes on a heavy sale in which case it is extremely worth the money.  Controls:  No notes, it's perfect. You get so many abilities and every single one of them functions and handles exactly how you'd think they should. It errs on the side of too lenient than not lenient enough which I think is exactly what this game needs.  Graphics:  I consider this one of the greatest looking games I've ever played. Some of the backgrounds for this game are drop dead gorgeous. Especially the visual themes for the land of the dead dimension made my jaw drop the first time I saw them. Every single enemy is visually distinct and have wonderful animations.  Difficulty:  This is where the game may stumble for some people. The short and sweet of is that around 80% of this game is relatively forgiving. However, I think part of why this is true is because of how well the game tutorializes their levels. The game slowly has you perform more and more complicated platforming challenges, each incorporating techniques learned in previous sections. This means that the game rarely throws more than 1 "new" challenge to you at any given time. That said, there are tricky sections, and I found them delightful. The vast majority of truly tricky areas are optional areas but I think the game is best played 100%ing it (and I rarely 100% games).  Story:   It's a non-story. Pretty goofy, but the characters which are present are silly and likable. It does what it needs to do in keeping the game moving. Nothing great here, but nothing I found to be annoying.  Replay value:   There is a hard mode which makes combat much more punishing, changes some enemy encounters to likewise be more difficult, and increases the speed of certain platforming hazards. Hard mode is definitely what you want to play after your first run, (it doesn't unlock until you beat the game once) and it's worth playing a second time or beyond.  Conclusion:  Hats off to DrinkBox. I got this in a random humble bundle I purchased, never thinking that it would someday become one of my favorite games of all time; but here we are. Thanks DrinkBox

전작인 1편 한글패치 제작했습니다. https://gall.dcinside.com/indiegame/204123         (아래는 한패 제작 전 작성했던 리뷰)     [h3] 이미 훌륭했던 전작을 더 맛있고 맵게 발전시킨 플랫포밍 갓겜 [/h3]  [b] 평가: 갓겜 [/b] 조작: 키보드 난이도 옵션: 초회차(보통), 싱글 플레이 플레이 타임: 16.5시간(中 DLC 2시간) 도전과제: 36/49   [b] 전작 상위호환 플랫포밍 갓겜. [/b] 1편은 재미보다는 완성도를 보고 갓겜이라고 평한 거라면, 2편은 순수하게 너무 재미있게 게임했다.   전작에서 아쉬웠던 챌린지, 스펙업 시스템, 미번역 이슈가 모두 개선됨. 챌린지는 엔딩과 별개의 컨텐츠가 되었고(다만 유료 DLC), 전작의 노히트 전투 같은 짜증나는 게 없어지고 개인적으로 훨씬 재미있는 스테이지들로 채워졌다. 스펙업 시스템은 전작의 상점보다 깔끔해졌고 스킨도 구매가 아닌 수집요소로 분리됨.  무엇보다 한글화가 된 게 컸다. 가끔 존댓말과 반말이 섞이는 이슈가 있긴 했지만 전반적으로는 번역 퀄리티가 괜찮았음. 덕분에 유머의 타율이 전편보다 훨씬 좋았고, 가끔 예상치 못하게 먹먹한 장면도 온전히 느낄 수 있어서 좋았다.   그렇다고 2편부터 하는 걸 추천하긴 어려움. 1편을 아예 안 할 거라면 모를까. 일단 2편 배경이 1편 결말에서 그대로 이어서 시작되는 식이라 이 게임 오프닝 자체가 전작 스토리의 중요한 스포가 됨.  그리고 액션 시스템과 난이도의 문제. 1편은 진엔딩 루트를 제외하고는 입문작으로 하기 좋은 쉬운 난이도였지만, 2편은 메인 진행에서도 상당히 어려운 부분이 많았다. 액션 시스템도 전작을 그대로 계승한 거에 몇 가지 새로운 요소를 추가한 식이라서 2편 뒤에 1편을 플레이한다면 너무 뻔하고 지루하게 느껴지기 딱일 듯.  그만큼 2편에서 발전된 플랫포밍 요소가 너무 맛있었다. '오리'가 떠오르는 배쉬 액션, 'VVVVVV'가 떠오르는 중력전환, 전작보다 발전한 닭 액션, 대쉬 초기화 등 추가된 요소들이 원래 기믹에 너무 잘 녹아듦. 덕분에 많이 매워지기는 했지만 재밌기는 진짜 재밌었다. 특히 배쉬 액션은 원조랑 비교해도 안 꿀릴 정도로 좋았음. 길찾기가 많이 쉬운 편이라 플랫포머 마니아들도 재밌게 할 듯.  (시작화면에서 코나미 커맨드 치면 초회차도 어려움 난이도로 플레이 가능. 전투와 플랫포밍 둘 다 어려워진다고 함.)   [b] 이미 훌륭했던 전작을 더 맛있고 맵게 발전시킨 플랫포밍 갓겜. [/b] 개인적으로 탑3 메트로배니아가 오리, 할나, 더 메신저였는데 여기에 끼워줘야겠음.

The platforming is some of the tightest and best "feeling" around, while the combat mechanics are deep enough that you can't just button mash your way through, but not so complex that they're intimidating or overly difficult to master.  Following the main path starts off being pretty easy and never really goes above providing just the right level of difficulty. Wander off said main path, however, and you'll be met with some pretty gruelling combat gauntlets and deviously tricky platforming segments. If you want to get the "good" ending, be prepared to have your skills tested.  This is also a fantastic looking game with fast and fluid animation and a lush art direction.  Guacamelee 2 is basically a second helping of everything that made the first game so great. If you enjoyed that first game or you enjoy Metroidvanias in general, you'll likely enjoy this.

I was able to 100% the game in around 10 hours or so on normal difficulty.  I played the first game and enjoyed it quite a bit.  This sequel is basically the exact same game with new levels and a different story.    The charm is still in the game and I found the platforming to be more challenging in this one.  In certain areas it felt like Celeste level of difficult platforming which was a tad bit more difficult then it needed to be.  Overall though the combat is fun,  the story is light, and if you liked the first this is more of what you had in the original.   On normal difficulty the game is easy,  with all the moves unlocked you can cruise through any of the fights.  The end boss and everything else was a cake walk.    I did not have a chance to try multiplayer,  I'm not sure if the levels would lend well for 4 players at the same time.  Lots of the areas are compact with tight platforming so I'd imagine it would get annoying.    Fun game overall and worth a purchase.

Guacamelee 2 is an amazing sequel to the first game. However, it does do some serious stumbling in other areas that keep it from reaching the true heights the series can achieve.  The Good: The writing, art direction and moment-to-moment gameplay are still brilliant. They even beefed up the chicken form to be more than a reason to squeeze through small pathways. It was also awesome to see how they worked in the previous characters and fan reaction to the first game. Then there are all the awesome easter eggs they've scattered in the game! It's really a love letter to video games. Runs like a dream on the Steam Deck.  The Bad: The controls can still be finicky, especially when you add the chicken form into the mix and now you've gotta hit your controller like a stenographer playing the game as it progresses. I honestly don't know why it didn't get more forgiving than the first one. There are also weird difficulty spikes. The devs will absolutely rake you over the coals for $400 then you'll fight a boss who dies to a kitten sneezing in their direction.  The Ugly: This game *fetishizes* one-hit deaths. It's criminal. By about the halfway mark of the game, the environment can kill you in one hit, enemies can kill you in one hit and enemies that explode anywhere on the screen can kill you in one hit. Then you get to the challenge levels and you just want to rip your face off as you're trying to pull off twitch platforming with Juan and literally a fraction of a second will cause you to lose. On *numerous* occasions Juan has refused to jump, attack, react, etc. despite clearly hitting the button. I've never felt so gaslit by a video game before. The challenge levels themselves aren't really fun - they're frustrating. I'm assuming that's a chicken/egg thing given the controls.  But for all the criticisms I have, I couldn't put the game down. If you're not a crazy completionist, this game is an easy recommend at any price. If you are a crazy completionist, just be aware that it can be a little bit frustrating when deaths aren't in your control - literally.

Продолжение из разряда "тех же щей, да погуще лей".  Кто не наигрался в первую часть -- поиграть можно. Кто не играл -- играйте в первую, там все очень классно с точки геймплея.  На сюжет было безразлично, а остальных аспектах почти все хорошо, как и в первой части.  Иногда перебор с количеством тектовых диалогов, но всякие словесные шуточки очень удачны в русской локализации и иногда вызывают улыбку.  Жаль, что игру для всех достижей без легального чит-кода, который сразу открывает тяжелую сложность, придется проходить 2 раза. От игры получил много удовольствия именно в первом прохождении, пройдя сюжет и зачистив все локации. Второй раз было проходить немножко нудновато, особенно когда ты знаешь все об игре, а сложность тебя пытается парить только бОльшим получаемым уроном. За исключением пары арен это вообще никакой роли не играет.  Иногда были проблемы со считываем ввода во время спец-приемов, но вроде не так часто, чтобы улетать на жопной тяге в стратосферу. Так, скорее была пара проблем на парочке испытаний и на одной арене.  Очень печально было видеть, что прилетев в храм испытаний без купленного длц, передо мной нагло открылся магазин Steam для покупки. Не хватало только надписи "Слышь, купи". Мне 29 рублей по скидке не жалко на длц, но сам формат продажи выглядел убого.  Но все равно, игру могу спокойно порекомендовать любителям жанра, если стилистика не вызывает отвращения.

Помните, как в детстве вы страдали, когда не могли пройти: последние миссии NFS Underground, в Prince of Persia WW локацию без основного оружие или же погони в Simpsons Hit & Run ? Так вот - это оно. Та самая игра, что заставит ваши последние нервные клетки снова трепетать.  В игре есть несколько интересных боссов типа Марионето (танцор) и Кактуса. Но последний босс не лучше, сделан, чем в предыдущей части - только накидывает урон отовсюду и необходимо молниеносно реагировать на его атаки.  Еще проблема в том, что иногда вы не можете найти своего персонажа среди огромного месива врагов на экране. Зон с файтингами в игре завались, как впрочем и парКУРных. И те и другие не радуют, если честно.  Про платформинг многие писали - это нечто на грани нервного срыва. Вам придется стать пианистом, но и это вас не спасет. Приготовьтесь потратить десятки попыток, приближающихся к сотне на то, чтобы пройти небольшой участок лишь затем, чтобы пройти следующий - более усложненный. Такое ощущение, что разрабы считают, что игрок каждую секунду прикладывает к монитору линейку, чтобы высчитать попиксельно каждое действие персонажа, ибо в этой игре -каждый пиксель на счету. Промахнулся на микромиллиметр - наткнулся на расширенную, невидимую зону урона убивающих шипов, либо недолетел, либо перелетел и так далее.  Да, игра стала лучше по всем параметрам, за курю наконец-то стало интересно играть, но именно тот факт, что игра не только осталась душным уничтожителем нервов, но и возвела это в абсолют - не позволяет накинуть положительную оценку. Честно, я бы даже среднюю не поставил, если бы она тут была. Несмотря на некоторые положительные эмоции, основной было жопное горение и от этого никуда не уйти.  Помимо этого люди пишут, что если вы не сохранитесь после подбора сундука с куском сердца/черепа и прочих важных плюшек, а затем умрете - вы потеряете этот кусок навсегда. Я зашел в комментарии до начала игры и потому избежал этих ошибок... А может быть в игре это пофиксили, но лично я не проверял.  Вот такая она, неоднозначная игра с реальным потенциалом, разбивающаяся о максимально глумящиеся, изнуряющие, душные платформинговые зоны вперемешку с файтами, где в каше врагов легко затеряться. Не знаю, будет ли 3 часть, но если да, то надеюсь, что в третий раз они не наступят на те же грабли...

[h2]Guacamelee! 2 - O Méxicovânia retorna...[/h2]  [b]Pontos Positivos[/b]:  [i]Gameplay divertido e desafiador[/i]  -O jogo mantém a fórmula de sucesso do primeiro título, com um combate fluido e satisfatório, combinado com movimentos de plataforma precisos e divertidos de executar.  -A introdução de novas habilidades e ataques mantém a jogabilidade fresca e envolvente.  [b]Design visual vibrante e único[/b]  -O estilo artístico inspirado na cultura mexicana continua impressionante, com cores vibrantes e cenários cheios de personalidade e bastante detalhados.  - A animação dos personagens e inimigos continua bem-feita, detalhada e cheia de charme.  [b]Humor e referências culturais[/b]  -E mais uma vez o jogo veio repleto de piadas inteligentes, referências à cultura pop e ao universo dos videogames, o que torna a experiência leve e divertida e que em alguns momentos me fizeram dar bastante risadas.  [b]Exploração e segredos[/b]  -O mundo do jogo continua repleto de áreas secretas, coletáveis e desafios extras, incentivando a exploração.  [b]Trilha sonora[/b]  -As músicas continuam bem inspiradas em ritmos mexicanos, combina perfeitamente com o clima do jogo e ajuda a imergir o jogador no universo do jogo.  ================================================================================================  [b]Pontos Negativos[/b]  [i]Pouca inovação em relação ao primeiro jogo[/i]  Embora o jogo seja divertido, ele não traz muitas novidades em relação ao seu predecessor. Quem jogou o primeiro título pode sentir que está vivendo uma experiência muito familiar.  [i]Dificuldade irregular[/i]  -Algumas seções de plataforma e chefes podem ser frustrantemente difíceis, o que chegava até a desanimar.  -A curva de dificuldade nem sempre é bem balanceada, com picos repentinos em certas fases.  [b]Repetitividade em alguns momentos[/b]  -Alguns puzzles e mecânicas de plataforma podem se tornar repetitivos ao longo do jogo, especialmente em fases mais longas.  -Limitado apelo para quem não conhece o primeiro jogo  -A belíssima PWTARIA de colocarem conquistas em conteúdo adicional. ou seja, se você procura fazer os 100%, vais ter que comprar as 2 DLC'S... Que eu não irei comprar! (Mas, aceito de presente. JJJJJJJJJJJK :v )   [b]Conclusão[/b]  [b]Guacamelee! 2[/b] é uma boa sequência que mantém a qualidade do primeiro jogo, com um gameplay divertido, um visual colorido, detalhado e um humor cativante. No entanto, a falta de inovação e alguns problemas de balanceamento de dificuldade podem ser pontos negativos para alguns. Agora o que me incomodou mesmo foi o meu ultimo ponto na crítica. Ainda assim, é uma excelente escolha para fãs de metroidvanias e jogos cooperativos(Que não testei)

Guacamelee! 2 es una explosión de color, acción y humor que te sumerge en un mundo inspirado en la vibrante cultura mexicana. En este juego, encarnas a un héroe lleno de carisma y habilidades de lucha que debe enfrentarse a enemigos en niveles repletos de plataformas, secretos y desafíos inesperados. La secuela mejora y expande la fórmula del primer título, combinando un diseño de mundo abierto con combates ágiles y precisos, todo ello aderezado con un estilo gráfico pixelado y lleno de vida. Su propuesta se posiciona de forma sobresaliente en el mercado del Metroidvania, compitiendo en la liga de clásicos modernos como [b]Hollow Knight[/b] y [b]Ori and the Blind Forest[/b], pero con ese toque irreverente y lleno de humor que la distingue. La crítica y la comunidad han aclamado su evolución y su capacidad para mantener la emoción en cada partida, convirtiéndolo en una experiencia adictiva y refrescante.  [b]Pros[/b]   [list] [*] [b]Jugabilidad Ágil y Desafiante:[/b] Los controles son precisos y el combate se siente fluido, permitiendo encadenar combos espectaculares y movimientos dinámicos que hacen que cada enfrentamiento sea emocionante. [*] [b]Estilo Visual Único:[/b] Su arte pixelado y la paleta de colores vibrante celebran la cultura mexicana, creando escenarios llenos de vida y detalles que resaltan en el género. [*] [b]Innovación en el Diseño de Niveles:[/b] Cada zona está cuidadosamente diseñada para sorprender al jugador, con rutas alternativas, secretos escondidos y desafíos que invitan a la exploración constante. [*] [b]Humor y Carácter Inconfundible:[/b] Los diálogos y situaciones están impregnados de un humor desenfadado y original, lo que le da al juego una personalidad única y cercana. [*] [b]Recepción Crítica Muy Positiva:[/b] Tanto la prensa especializada como la comunidad han elogiado la evolución de la secuela, destacándola por su equilibrio entre dificultad, diversión y originalidad. [/list]  [b]Contras[/b]   [list] [*] [b]Dificultad Elevada en Algunos Tramos:[/b] La exigencia de ciertos niveles puede resultar frustrante para jugadores menos experimentados, obligando a una curva de aprendizaje considerable. [*] [b]Narrativa Superficial:[/b] Si bien el enfoque está en la acción y la exploración, la historia se cuenta de forma mínima, lo que puede dejar a algunos deseando una trama más profunda, en comparación con otros Metroidvania que invierten en narrativa. [*] [b]Repetitividad Ocasional:[/b] A pesar de la variedad de entornos, algunos jugadores podrían sentir cierta monotonía tras largas horas de juego, similar a lo que ocurre en títulos con mecánicas muy definidas. [*] [b]Curva de Aprendizaje Pronunciada:[/b] La combinación de acción rápida y exploración requiere adaptarse rápidamente, lo que puede ser un reto para quienes se inician en el género. [/list]  [b]Conclusión[/b]   Guacamelee! 2 es una apuesta segura para quienes buscan una experiencia metroidvania llena de energía, humor y un inconfundible toque mexicano. Su jugabilidad adictiva, su estilo visual encantador y su diseño de niveles innovador hacen que cada partida sea una aventura única. Aunque su dificultad y una narrativa algo minimalista puedan representar un desafío para algunos, las virtudes del juego –tal como lo han destacado la crítica y la comunidad– superan cualquier pequeño inconveniente. En definitiva, si te apasionan los juegos de acción y plataformas que combinan nostalgia con innovación, Guacamelee! 2 es, sin duda, un título que no querrás dejar de jugar.

my playtime is low because I play on a modified exe that enables ultrawide support. my true gametime is closer to 2-3 hours. kind of dumb that the game doesnt allow this in its settings because it only takes changing 4 bytes in a hex editor and works basically flawlessly... anyways...  I just dont find this game particularly fun. the visual design is incredible, and the combat is pretty decent, but there is so much *annoying* design here that it kind of just drags the experience down. the sound design is ROUGH. so many annoying repetitive sounds, particularly in pollo mode (there is maybe one or two very similar sounds PERIOD for when you attack, and you spend lots of time in pollo mode as the ability to fight in it like normal is one of the new features of the game). thats a huge issue but I will just list the rest out to not spend too much of my time complaining:  - traversal abilities and combat abilities are one in the same, this isnt an issue on its own but these abilities are on a cooldown which can severely slow things down.  - the humor is very hit or miss, mostly miss for me. its very 'meme centric', and rife with lolfunnyrandom. its got a bit of the borderlands problem.  - on my screen the black bar at the top of the screen is literally an inch and a half tall. an inch and a half of nothingness constantly covering up the games beautiful art that only serves to flash 'p2/3/4 press start to join' text while you play the game. its a petty nitpick maybe, but its so big and only serves the multiplayer which I have no intention to engage with so its kind of annoying. im like 99% sure they make it smaller in the store screenshots which is weird... - this game feels more platforming/traversal heavy and less combat heavy than the first game which is not to the experience's strength imo. just a ton of running around all the time and the platforming isnt all that great to begin with. - there is an in-game area you can get to but cant do anything in unless you buy the dlc, which a character in-game tells you to go buy. for some reason this is just weird and kind of annoying in a metroidvania game. I would have preferred it just not be on the map if I dont own said dlc.

Los plataformas bien hechos son de los juegos que merecen la pena jugar. Este es un intento de plataforma que lucha con la falta de precisión en los controles. Pensarías que habiendo hecho un juego anteriormente mejorarías la segunda parte, pues no, más de lo mismo. Escenarios colmados de enemigos y obstáculos que solo alargan más la carencia de historia. No puedes seleccionar dificultad, por lo que si quieres completar todos los logros, debes volver a jugar todo de nuevo. Los escenarios, parecen llenos de vida, pero no, sala tras sala de nada.  Cuando juegas piensas "esteré lo suficientemente preparado para el boss final", tomas la decisión de explorar para juntar oro. Al finalizar te das cuenta que ese tiempo explorando fue un desperdicio, ya que los patrones del boss son muy simples, no representan un reto (Me bastó morir una vez para aprender todos los patrones)   En términos generales, Este juego tienen todo para ser un ben juego, pero lo tira todo por la borda para ser un juego soporífero, extremadamente largo, donde hay mucho que explorar pero a la vez no hay nada interesante que ver.

Una secuela hecha y derecha, digo esto por si no jugaron al primer [b]Guacamelee[/b], la versión [b]Gold[/b] o la [b]Super Turbo Chamipship Edition[/b], se los recomiendo. Más y mejor, con el mismo humor y mejoras como que luchar como pollo sea viable a diferencia del primero, nuevos enemigos que lo vuelven más desafiante y mayor progresión de habilidades.  Incluso tiene un gancho para las secciones de plataformas, si te lo pones a pensar, esto por ahora es lo más cercano a tener [b]Hollow Knight: Silksong[/b] ya que salió con años de diferencia. Por no decir que cuando subí esta reseña todavía no salió ese otro juego.  Las reseñas negativas de este juego se resumen en «es un juego muy difícil» que si bien es más desafiante, para los estándares de un [i]metroidvania[/i] el primero era bastante sencillo y fácil, no sé si por eso la gente se queja tanto, porque personalmente [b]Blasphemous[/b] y [b]Hollow Knight[/b] me parecen más complicados que [b]Guacamelee 2[/b], aunque no jugué en el modo difícil para decir si es injusto, lo único que se me hizo muy complicado aunque desafiante a su manera eran las secciones de plataforma para sacar el final bueno.  En lo personal no me gustaron tanto los villanos como en el primer juego, no sé si es cosa mía o si alguien más sintió lo mismo.  Algo que me da risa de este juego es que viajamos por distintas dimensiones y hay varias que son bastantes únicas, por ejemplo en una está «la dimensión del grindeo» que lo vuelve un juego por turnos, me hubiera gustado ver más dimensiones pero las que hay son graciosas.  Cosas malas: 1) No tenes el modo INTENSO como en [b]Guacamelee Super Turbo Championship Edition[/b]. 2) Hay opciones visuales que no me gustaron como el “motion blur”, pero específicamente la de los destellos blanco al recibir un golpe es justamente la única que no se puede desactivar. 3) Si esperas más exploración que su antecesor te tengo malas noticias: El juego es todavía más lineal que antes.

"Barely" recommend on sale less than $10.   Guacamelee 2 is a disappointing regurgitation of the first game with little innovation, control problems, and linear level design masked as a metroidvania. If you would enjoy more levels to Guacamelee 1, then you may enjoy this. For everyone else, there are many better options out there.    PROS: -Art style -Underlying combat and movement system is fundamentally good but expect to be frustrated because moves simply won't register correctly. (I did not have this problem with the first game).  NEUTRALS: -Comedy style of the first game is getting predictable and played out.  -Guacamelee 2 is just Guacamelee 1 but "more". There is little to no innovation - mostly repetition.   CONS: -Several of the upgrades are literally just "+damage" to a move. These types of upgrades add nothing and are extremely lazy.  -UNSKIPPABLE cutscenes/dialog.  -Poor level design.  Although on the surface it looks like a typical metroidvania map, in reality every single area has the exact same linear design with branching bonus rooms and a shortcut path.  -Clunky Controls. I can't count how many times I jumped towards an enemy and did a neutral special only for the game to register side special.  Single hop down+special is practically impossible (it simply won't register). Doing a wall run immediately after hitting the wall has a strange timing - if you get it right you can do it really fast, but if you miss it, the game simply ignores the inputs for ~1 second before he starts running. There are no audio or visual cues to tell you that.   -Terrible balance. Final boss is the easiest boss of the game. The "true ending" challenge rooms are disproportionately harder than the rest of the game and one of them has no checkpoints.  -Hard Mode was a complete afterthought. There are several combat rooms that are so small / overcrowded that there is almost no room to move. As a result, you have to memorize the enemy spawn patterns and kill them as they spawn or else it is literally impossible to win because there is nowhere to move.

Загальне враження: Ця гра мене приємно здивувала! Я взагалі люблю метроїдванії, і це безперечно один з яскравих і унікальних представників жанру.  Детальніше: Перші пару годин гри видались дуже простими і я вже навіть думав закинути її, але чим далі тим складнішим стає платформінг, нові здібності додають не лише нові атаки, але і додатвові способи переміщення. Окреме пекло це квест по збору ключа для курок, там всі челенджі змусили попітніти! Система бою в грі доволі проста, деякі моменти в керуванні не ідеальні, але все це компенсується цікавим і складним платформінгом. А цікавий візуальний стиль і гумор створюють приємну атмосферу. Також є можливість підключити до 4 гравців, що мабуть ледь не єдиний приклад коопу в такому жанрі. Вердикт: маст плей якщо маєте міцні нерви і готові довго і багато стрибати, кувиркатися, бігати по стінам і оце все що ми так любимо ;)

Guacamelee! 2 es la secuela de el grandísimo metroidvania Guacamelee. Una obra maestra que aúna plataformas, mamporros, mejoras, exploración, coleccionables y un humor excepcional.  Es difícil estar a la altura de su predecesor, ya que era casi perfecto. En esta segunda parte encontramos nuevos enemigos, bosses y poderes, a la vez que encontramos viejos conocidos de la entrega anterior. Le da mucho más protagonismo a la forma de pollo y a los movimientos plataformeros encadenados.  En definitiva, una buena manera de tener más Guacamelee, más horas de diversión con el Luchador más duro del Mexiverso. ¡Una pasada!

The game offers a lot in both its combat (lots of attacks to use, with enemy weaknesses tied to specific abilities) and its platforming (multiple mechanics all offering a combination of different challenges).  I completed the game solo, so there were some moments in the combat encounters where I was challenged to think about my approach as opposed to button mashing. What was unexpectedly challenging for me were the platforming puzzles - given the vast amount of abilities offered to the player, the game provides a lot of ways to push those abilities to the limit.  This game is mostly linear in the sense that backtracking and revisiting of previous areas doesn't happen as aggressively as other Metroidvania titles. The game's premise is fun and quirky, but personally I wasn't expecting much depth in terms of storytelling as the dialogue and cutscenes lean a lot on video game references and light humour. Basically, the game isn't taking itself too seriously on that front.  Overall - a solid game. It scratched the itch I had to play a 2D beat-em-up with 'Smash Bros.'-like mechanics, but offered a lot more in the form of its platforming puzzles.

[h1]Guacamelee 2: Combos Perfectos, pero con un Regreso Algo Pesado[/h1]  Guacamelee 2 brilla con su jugabilidad fluida y ágil, combinando combos de lucha libre y habilidades especiales con la precisión de una buena cumbia. La mezcla de combate y exploración estilo "metroidvania" mantiene el interés, con habilidades nuevas que abren caminos a lo largo del juego.  Pero, si planeas completarlo al 100%, el backtracking puede ser frustrante. Los niveles son grandes, y solo hay un portal por zona, lo que hace que tengas que recorrer grandes distancias sin muchos atajos. Esto corta un poco el ritmo y te deja con la sensación de estar repitiendo la misma canción, pero sin el mismo gusto.  Aún así, es una experiencia sólida y desafiante, perfecta para quienes disfrutan de un buen reto... y un buen guacamole.  [h2]Puntuación ★★★★✬ 4.5/5[/h2]

L’intera trama del Mexiverso è sull’orlo della distruzione, il ritorno di un eroe mascherato porta una nuova speranza…  La storia prende vita molti anni dopo la sconfitta del malvagio Calaca grazie agli sforzi del potente Juan, il luchador che salvò il regno dei Vivi e quello dei Morti da un funesto rituale che rischiava di distruggere il ciclo della vita e della morte. Dopo essersi fidanzato con l’incantevole Lupita e aver avuto due splendidi figli, il nostro Juan riceve la visita inaspettata di Tostada (lo spirito della maschera che lo salvò dalla morte nel primo capitolo della serie) che lo avverte che tutte le realtà del Mexiverso stanno per essere distrutte dal crudele piano di Salvador, leggendario luchador che sconfisse Calaca nella linea temporale più oscura (in cui Juan e Lupita sono stati uccisi nel corso del rituale avvenuto al Grande Tempio) e che, corrotto dal potere della sua maschera, vuole impadronirsi dei manufatti necessari per gustare il Guacamole Sacro, leggendario cibo creato da antiche divinità in grado di conferire poteri così terribili da disintegrare tutte le realtà del Mexiverso, costringendo il nostro Juan a indossare nuovamente la sua maschera e ritornare sulla scena nei suoi panni di luchador per sventare i folli piani di Salvador e del suo gruppo di malvagi.  Prepariamoci a un nuovo viaggio nel pittoresco e spettacolare mondo di Guacamelee 2, seguendo il nostro Juan mentre esplorerà le sconfinate e variegate zone della linea temporale più oscura per intralciare i piani di potere assoluto di Salvador e dei suoi tirapiedi, affrontando vecchie e nuove orde di nemici e potenti Boss pronti a farci a pezzi e a vanificare i nostri sforzi nel corso di frenetici combattimenti d’azione in ambienti 2D, sessioni di platform estremo in cui muoverci tra le dimensioni dei Vivi e dei Morti per procedere con la storia principale e nuove arene di combattimento in cui scatenare il nostro arsenale di attacchi terra/aria, prese di wrestling, schivate e i poteri mistici delle statue per rientrare in possesso del montate cedrone, del diretto fulmineo, della testata olmec e delle altre mosse per superare gli ostacoli nelle varie zone del mondo, spezzare gli scudi difensivi dei nostri insidiosi avversari e completare le fasi esplorative in ogni angolo degli ambienti per accumulare fiumi di monete per sbloccare nuove abilità, frammenti di cuore per accrescere la salute e teschi per aumentare la nostra resistenza, portando a termine alcune sfide per guadagnare il rispetto di potenti allenatori/allenatrici che saranno disposti a insegnarci nuove capacità passive, tecniche inedite e potenziamenti per il nostro arsenale di mosse, trasformandoci nella leggendaria forma di Pollo per accedere a nuove zone nascoste, entrare in contatto con una strana setta e rivendicare nuovi poteri per seminare terrore e distruzione tra le file nemiche, superando con successo tutte le prove sul nostro cammino e raggiungendo così l’antagonista di turno per dimostrare una volta per tutte chi è il vero luchador del Mexiverso, gustandoci tutte le nuove vicende di una trama che combina con grande stile dimensioni parallele, momenti epici, siparietti comici e colpi di scena a non finire.   Guacamelee 2 è il secondo e ultimo capitolo della serie fantasy d’azione platform in salsa metroidvania 2D che ci porterà a vivere una nuova avventura nelle infinite trame del Mexiverso in cui le forze del Bene e quelle del Male si scontreranno ancora una volta per decidere il destino di ogni realtà conosciuta nel corso delle 13 ore necessarie per arrivare alla fine delle vicende principali (escludendo il tempo aggiuntivo per ottenere il 100% della mappa di gioco, completare la modalità difficile e portare a termine le storie secondarie per assistere al finale segreto della trama), assaporando tutte le meccaniche di un titolo apprezzabile su pc fissi o portatili di fascia bassa/media (con almeno 2 gb di vram video dedicata per non incappare in sospensioni improvvise del gioco con conseguenti arresti dei computer) e godibile su sistemi operativi di livello alto/gaming senza bug, glitch e problemi strani.   Voto Personale 88/100

Guacamelee 2 continues the trend of the first game of having fun combat, challenging platforming and a humorous cast of characters. There aren't too many new abilities in this one, but I loved Guacamelee 1 and this is more of it.   As other reviews have said, this isn't really a metroidvania since it's actually quite linear in progression. That's not necessarily a bad thing though, since the world design doesn't lend itself to quick traversal even after finding fast travel points. I was also a little disappointed there still aren't any colorblindness options considering all the many-colored shields you have to break with color-coded moves.   Aside from a few minor complaints, it's an enjoyable game and it's worth a few hours of playtime. Perfect game for Steam deck, too.

Guacamelee 2 is a fun and fast-paced Metroidvania. You unlock several abilities through the course of the game that are used during combat and unlock paths to new locations. It has an interesting mechanic where you will find coloured blocks that will block your path and you will have to use the relevant ability to break it, this helps you to read the map and reduce backtracking, ensuring you will only go back to places that you really need to go.  The combat is fast-paced and is not hard at all, the toughest challenges are - actually - the Platformer aspct of the game, and only if you are planning to do the true end and/or 100% completion. One of the issues with the combat in my option is the hit-box, for some reason I feel it is so weird and it makes the combat not as responsive as I whiched for. As for its aesthetic, I found it quite refreshing and funny. It has lots of homages to different famous games and lots of "memes", but I really loved was the Mexican background  - though I would say it relies on stereotypes - I find funny and I loved the occasional Spanish word that appear here and there during the conversation. You can play this game coach co-op up to 4 players, I never did that but I can only imagine it would be mayhem - which could be quite cool - similarity to Magicka.  It goes without it is an upgrade over Guacamelee 1, improving on every aspect of the game.
