[h1]MetroidVania e Coop ideia boa e execução frustrante[/h1] O jogo e super divertido nas primeiras 3horas, após isso se torna um inferno...  comprei o jogo para mim e minha namorada jogarmos juntos e nos divertirmos ja que e um genero que nos dois amamos, não me entenda mal o jogo e absurdamente lindo e muito criativo em diversos topicos, porém a ma funcionabilidade do cooperativo (seja de 4 a 2 pessoas) não funciona, varios dos puzzles feitos no jogo e pensado para somente 1 jogador executalas, enquanto os outros assistem mortinhos.  Sobre Puzzles, com passar o tempo e normal que vc ganhe habilidades para te ajudar a avançar no jogo, enquanto ele vai se tornando mais dificil, fazendo com que seja recompensador a dificuldade para passar em um puzzle, a mesma mecanica acontece aqui so que de uma forma mais massiva e desinteressante. enquanto hollow knight vc pode passar uma tarde tentando passar por caminho absurdo de dificil para pegar uma larvinha onde caso queira você pode simplesmente ignorar, aqui não, VOCÊ vai ficar a tarde toda tendo que pegar um coração que e um item necessário para melhorar seu personagem, e isso se torna frustante no momento em que os desafios so ficam extremamente dificeis enquanto os controles não ajudam nem um pouco sendo eles lentos no tempo de resposta ou só não querendo funcionar.  POWER UPS, os power ups são muito bonitos eu gostei da criatividade em relação a nomeação dos golpes e como são usados, minha critica a eles e que eles parecem mais um atraso do que uma mão na roda, um exemplo de power up que pra mim e uma das piores e o salto no tempo, Nesse jogo você consegue pular de linhas temporas para resolver alguns puzzles intercalando entre o mundo dos mortos e dos vivos, Uma ideia incrivel ne?? CLARO. Horrorosa no momento em que e executado de maneira horrivel, vc tem 2 opçoes para saltar no tempo:  Ficando cinza; onde você manda somente o seu personagem para a outra linha do tempo  Mudando a tela; onde todo o cénario muda indicando qual linha do tempo está  O problema e que, ao fazer isso as paredes tambem mudam de lugar ou apenas somem, seria uma mecanica divertida, se toda vez que vc muda de "tela" ela não pincasse 3 mil vezes te deixando confuso e tonto.

You either get on board with the indulgent comedy or filter out what's going on.  That said, if you're after literally any other aspect of it, you're in for a treat. Combat is as satisfying as the first game on average. There are ups and downs on that point, but overall it's great. The platforming is top notch - a wonderful refinement of the same formula. The "post-game" is appreciably more accessible - less inscrutable puzzle platforming for those who want to pour a lot of time into the game to really earn another ending, more straightforward engaging challenges. The soundtrack is on point, and the visuals are solid.

Guacamelee! 2 is a handheld (Nintendo Switch)/console game that's also been put on Steam as a bit of an afterthought, as it's very much designed for the handheld/console gaming audience.  It's the sequel to Guacamelee, the luchador comedy brawler metroidvania.   On the upside, the game does feature customisable controls and resolution, so at least some of the basic, minimum requirements have been met.  Unfortunately there's a number of other shortcomings which contribute to the game being difficult to recommend to gamers.  The game works but it's imperfect, with remarkably steep difficulty in some areas, somewhat contrived "metroidvania" components, and sometimes equally contrived comedy writing (and perhaps a bit too much fourth wall/metagame humour).   While the game is only 2D, it's fairly well put together for a 2D game, doesn't feature ugly pixel "art" or any other laziness.  Visuals are improved a fair bit from the previous game with higher quality assets and visual effects in use.   Because this is designed for handheld/portable gaming appliances, it carries a number of deliberate design deficiencies.  Compromises were made to cater to the low power handheld gaming appliances that the game was designed for.  These are unfortunate compromises and limitations that PC gamers shouldn't be forced to accept, but it's evident that PC was a second thought for the mobile game developers.  The game is deficient as a result of these choices, and would have been so much better without the handicaps that designing games for low power handhelds forces upon a game.  While this might run well on a Steam Deck, we must remember the vast majority of gamers here are gaming on PC.  So, should you buy this game?  Is this one of the best of the 100,000+ games on Steam?  One of the questions I often ask when a game gets pretty close to being recommendable is whether I would seriously engage in discussion with my friends, find out what they're playing lately and say "Stop, you need to put down GTA:V/Elden Ring/Counterstrike 2 and play this instead."  I couldn't say that in the case of Guacamelee 2.   Guacamelee! 2 has the utterly unrealistic price of $20 USD, for which you could easily get any number of older AAA games.  For comparison, the $20 asking price for this game could get you games like "Doom Eternal", "The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt" or "Mirror's Edge".  Quality, professionally made games like those are frequently on sale cheaper than this.  I think the asking price is a bit high for what is an "okay" game but not a great one.  Unfortunately Steam reviewers aren't presented with a "Maybe" option... if they did, that's what I'd choose.  This isn't a completely bad game, but it's also not one of the best.  As always, if you read my review and you can overlook the negatives I've called out, or they just aren't important to you, then by all means go ahead and buy this.

[h1]Guacamelee! 2: ¡Luchando con Estilo en un Mexiverso Colorido![/h1]  [h2]Resumen[/h2] Imagina que el día más surrealista de tu vida incluyera máscaras de lucha libre, pollos locos y una dimensiones alternativas. ¡Pues bienvenido a Guacamelee! 2! Este juego te pide que salves al Mexiverso mientras desempeñas el papel de Juan Aguacate, un luchador de lucha libre con más carisma que muchos influencers de Instagram. Si buscas diversión, acción a raudales y mucha salsa picante, esto es lo tuyo, amiguis.  [h2]Aspectos importantes e interesantes[/h2] - **Acción Enchilada**: De verdad que este juego pica, pero de la buena manera. Las mecánicas de combate son fluidas, intuitivas y satisfactoriamente picantes. - **Visualmente Sabroso**: Los gráficos y la animación son como un festín visual de Día de Muertos, lleno de color y estilo que te harán decir ¡Viva México! - **Cumbia hasta en la sopa**: La música es pegajosa hasta decir basta. Si no te sorprendes tarareando las melodías del juego mientras preparas tacos, no estás jugando correctamente.  [h2]Aspectos por mejorar[/h2] - **Perdido en la Traducción**: A veces, los chistes y referencias super específicas pueden hacer que si no eres del terruño, te pierdas un poco en la historia. - **Más Piñatas, por favor**: Aunque hay bastante contenido, a lo mejor un DLC no estaría mal para alargar la fiesta mexicana un poquito más. Vuelve todo el folk mexicano y en ocasiones es un cague de risa.   Además contamos con las mazmorras del pollo, ahora da más gusto combear y hacer especiales con la forma pollo, cosa que en primero no se podía.   No sé si haber jugado el anterior y platinado muy recientemente hizo que lo viera un poco más fácil que el primero pero eso no quiere decir que para alguien ajeno a la saga le parezca sencillo, al contrario, tiene su dificultad pero Juan, el prota, se maneja tan bien que da gusto llevarlo.  Una pena que para poder sacarle el 100% del juego hay que tener los DLCS, recomiendo mucho Guacamelee 2, pero de ser posible, comprarlo completo con y todo y DLCs.  Y una vez más, Guacamelee 2 es un juegazo que no se separa mucho del original pero que añade cosas que lo mejoran bastante, es relativamente corto y te tienta demasiado rejugarlo, lo veo totalmente recomendable más si te gustan metroidvanias, personalmente sí me emocionaría con un Guacamelee! 3.

You don't NEED to play the first game to understand this one, but it is highly recommended.  You have most of the same moves as last time (1 new chicken one), but they are strung together more elaborately than they ever were in the first game. I've seen a lot of reviews saying that this game is a little bit better than, but almost equal in quality to the original. I'm here to tell you that this is FAR from the case.   The original game established the formula, which was pretty fun. A solid beat-em-up, combined with Metroidvania-style unlocks in a 2D platformer. The reason this is such a good combination, is that finding a new power-up has a lot of implications. Not only can you access more areas of the map, but another layer of complexity is added to both the combat and the platforming (for example, the uppercut acts as a quasi-double jump, breaks red enemy shields, and launches enemies into the air). The Mexican aesthetic and goofy tone tie it all up in a nice little ribbon.  I can see why the two games are likened in quality-- the sequel uses pretty much the same formula. However, every single little refresh here adds A LOT to the game. This game blew my moderate expectations out of the water. I can't fully explain why this game feels 100x better than its predecessor, but it definitely does. It might boil down mostly to the DRASTICALLY improved level design. Boss fights especially (I can only speak for Hard mode since I used the Konami code), are actually FUN this time around. There is increased enemy and move variety. Chaining together your entire repertoire of moves to solve the combat arenas and perfectly-crafted precision platforming puzzles feels ridiculously satisfying. Secrets are well-hidden, but not obtuse (I only had to look up one chest to reach 100%). The challenging bits will make you feel determined rather than annoyed. Even the writing and the music are much stronger this time around.   If you kinda liked the first one, but not enough to pick this one up, I urge you to give Guac 2 a try anyway. If you've never played the first one, I would argue that there's no need to-- the sequel catches you up Telenovela-style on the events of the first. If you like tight platforming challenges, and enjoy the feeling of getting progressively more and more badass, then you'll probably find a lot to love here.  9.5/10

マップが５割程度埋まった所で投稿です。  最初はキャラクターのコミカルさにつられて購入しましたが 楽しめたのは序盤の方だけでした。  アクションを行う上で要求されるボタンの種類が多すぎて (回避、弱攻撃、技攻撃、掴み、世界切替、牽引フックのようなもの・・他メニュー画面など) おやじゲーマーにはストレス以外の何物でもありませんでした。  序盤以降、数部屋進むごとに数種のアクションを瞬時に使い分けながら 罠をクリアしていかないといません。 何度も何度も何度も何度も何度も何度も何度も・・・ だんだんとイライラがつのり途中で投げ出しました。  そういったゲームなんだと言われてしまえば それまでですが 私には操作性の悪さも相まって、このゲームが嫌いになってしまいました。  具体的には コンボやアクションの途中である程度レバーニュートラルにしないと反応しません。 想像して欲しいのですが  昇竜拳コマンドを入力する際レバーを途中でニュートラルにしなければいけないような感じです。  そんな事は問題ない　パラッパラッパー得意だよという方ならば このゲームを楽しめるかもしれません。  良いところは陽気な音楽と、死んでもすぐ近くで復活してリトライが容易な事です。  それでは皆さんさようなら

Tepid recommendation for a competently made sequel but if you're itching for more Guacamelee I'd recommend just replaying the first. Gameplay has very few alterations, which is fine because the original formula wasn't broken. However everything else has taken a step back here. The humor made me chuckle a few times but nowhere near as much as the original. The story is nowhere near as compelling mainly due to the fact that the characters are extremely one note here. The little character development there is is for alternate reality versions of existing characters that you don't really care about since they're not "your" versions of the characters. The villains backstory/motivation doesn't get explained until 30 minutes before the end and is nowhere near as interesting as the villain of the original title. Pacing of the story also suffers from some overly drawn out levels, which was one of the few complaints I had about the super turbo championship edition of the original game. The music also feels like a significant step down which was very disappointing. Hoping there's some major revitalization of this series if they try to revisit it again. It's decent but nothing to write home about.

Hello friends,  Today i will review the game Guacamelee! 2. This is the sequel to the original game Gucamelee 1, which is either gold or super turbo champion version, depending on which version you have played. I suggest getting the Super Turbo Championship Edition as it expands on the original game and includes the original version of the game. This game is similiar in story like into the spiderverse, as it takes place in multiple timelines. It is great metroidvania game basess on cultural aspects from mexico and spain. The hero is features is a Luchador, a type of wrestler. It features are much like it's predecessor game, so if you like the orignal version, make sure to pick up the second game too.  Some of the platforming is awesome and you may die alot doing some of them. It can be frustrating in some places, so it may require multiple deaths to get thru different sections until you get it right. The powers are pretty funny and awesome too. Many reviews say it improved on the original games playing, which i agree to and it has definetly improved it. Anyways, here is my pros and cons list:   PROS:  - A great metriodvania that explores the culture and aspects of mexican and spanish traditions. - Silly things related to other games and meme's. - Great music score. - Works on Windows 11 OS. - Controller are supported and recommended for the game. I used a switch NEO controller.  CONS:  - Game seems short for what it is. - Sometimes my controller did not respond when i put combos together and got stuck in a direction and not responded right away. It acted like a delay.  I do recommend it as it is quite fun for a metriodvania. It is also not too difficult, but some areas are challenging enough it may take several times to get the item or chest in the area.

Yeah, again. Fun little game, but a far cry fro Guacamelee 1. The story feels forced and gameplay lacks flow (it's basically just lucha after lucha after platforming puzzle after lucha... ad nauseaum). And the the puzzles and gauntlets are also really obnoxious - they're basically an exercise of memorising what you should do and in what order, and you discover that by trial and error (that is, by dying and trying again). Not a lot of improv room in there. Still the same dad jokes all over too. So yeah, if you just care about smacking things in decent metroidvania style, go for it. I don't regret my purchase, but won't replay. And speaking of which, don't play on normal - it's a waste of time. Do the cheat code in the title screen and start on hard. It's not exactly hard anyway.

The first 8 hours, I was in love with this game; the next 2 hours made me hate it. Initially, the game was enjoyable – I liked the combat, the skill tree progression, the simple storyline, and the quirky characters. The graphics lacked variety, but the style was cool. The platforming in the first 8 hours was engaging and diverse, with the ability to switch between Juan and the chicken, jumping between different dimensions – innovative features that truly made the gameplay unique and captivating.  However, at some point, the level designers seem to grow devilish horns. They construct levels in a way that forces you to painfully navigate a series of nerve-wracking obstacles. At each subsequent turn, a trap emerges that is impossible to pass on the first try. As a result, you die and respawn at the frustrating beginning of the location, compelled to set agility records with your fingers to reach the next new twist and, inevitably, meet your demise.  With great regret, I realized that, after a certain point, more cortisol is released in my blood than dopamine. In summary, the game might appeal to those with very nimble fingers or those who enjoy grinding through the same locations a hundred times.

故事虽然老套但有趣 墨西哥风格的画面和音乐有一种独特的魅力，音画方面我给满分 地图设计给满分，游玩起来很流畅，合理安排的收集品也让人有探索欲望，很不错的银河城地图规划。  缺点是某些流程必经的战斗和收集解密难度过高了，加上游戏内复杂的动作系统，变鸡变人、阴阳间场景切换，困难到了让人觉得设计师在故意刁难恶意满满的程度，要知道我通关的是普通模式而已，都恨不得把手柄砸设计师脑袋上，很难想象困难乃至更高难度是什么地狱图景。  总结的话还是瑕不掩瑜，除去小部分因难度过高导致的无能狂怒，游戏整体带给我的游玩体验还是很不错的，市面上的杂牌银河城应该多学学这游戏的地图设计。 如果有下作，希望制作组能好好平衡下普通难度和硬核难度，起码要分出游乐玩家和硬核玩家的区别，别在必经流程里放那些难度过高的内容了，游戏带给玩家的更多应该是快乐和满足，而不是愤怒和咒骂(设计越变态就越兴奋的硬核抖S除外)。

If you've played Guacamelee 1, this is more of the same, but better in pretty much every way.  However, this is not a game for beginners.  Some of the later challenges are brutal, especially the Proving Grounds and almost everything related to the Special Key Pieces.  Secret-wise, nothing is obtuse and you just need to look at the map and explore a bit.  However, getting everything is another matter.  Most extra upgrades and money are locked behind various challenges and this is the meat of Guacamelee 2.  Fortunately, checkpoints are very generous.  If you're looking for a hardcore challenge, this game is for you.  As far as complaints, the control scheme can get very involved and sometimes you'll have to think about what button to press.  Also, combos involving the Y button like the eagle toss and later mobility upgrades can be a little finicky at times.

Не шедевр, не маст плей, а просто более-менее сносный платформер с бесполезной боёвкой и в основном опциональным бэктрекингом на нормале. На харде - как босиком по гравийке: каждый косяк разрабов чувствуется.    БОЁВКА:  Аще себя никак не проявляет на нормале. Что она есть, что её нету. Тупо спамишь спешиалами, пока стамина не кончится. Потом откатываешься, ждёшь её полного регена - повторяешь. К середине появляются враги, которые более-менее стоят на ногах после такого, но и от них тоже не оч много проблем: к этому времени уже можно найти/купить кучу апгрейдов здоровья и стамины, что сводит весь вражеский урон к минимуму.  На сложном ситуация - обратная. Всё - зашибись, пока не доходишь до 2-го босса. Тут уже начинаются вопросы по типу "А почему при прожатии спешилов, у гг нет кадров неуязвимости? Чтоб игроки не спамили сильными атаками? Но ведь за это отвечает стамина? Хорошо, а зачем тогда на экране столько врагов, которые перекрывают своими спрайтами друг друга? Как мне увидеть, что враги замахиваются? Как аще понять, что во время прожатия спешлов, можно получить дамаг, если в этот момент всего гг окутывает сияющая аура, которая обычно в играх символизирует, что ему - пох на весь урон? ПОЧЕМУ НЕЛЬЗЯ ПОВОРАЧИВАТЬСЯ ВО ВРЕМЯ КОМБО? ПОЧЕМУ ГГ ДОЛЖЕН СТОЯТЬ РОВНО ПЕРЕД ВРАЖИНОЙ, ЧТОБЫ ПРОШЁЛ УРОН? ПОЧЕМУ ОН НЕ ПРОХОДИТ ЕСЛИ СПРАЙТЫ НАКЛАДЫВАЮТСЯ ДРУГ НА ДРУГА ДАЖЕ ЧУТЬ-ЧУТЬ? ПОЧЕМУ, МАТЬ ВАШУ, АППЕРКОТ ИДЁТ ПО ДУГЕ, А НЕ ПРОСТО ВВЕРХ? ВЕДЬ МНЕ ТАК ХОЧЕТСЯ ПРОСРАТЬ ЯЧЕЙКУ СТАМИНЫ, ПОТОМУ ЧТО Я ПРОМАЗАЛ ПО ВРАГУ И СВАЛИЛСЯ ВНИЗ, ГДЕ МЕНЯ УЖЕ ЖДЁТ КУЧА ПРОДЖЕКТАЙЛОВ, ЛЕТЯЩИХ СКВОЗЬ СТЕНЫ.     ПЛАТФОРМИНГ:  У меня к платформингу вопросов почти нет. Я даже жалею, что полез на хард и испортил себе всё впечатление об игре, ибо попрыгушки, за некоторым исключением, тащили на себе всё моё первое прохождение. В основном, если геймдизайнер был в хорошем настроении, то эти этапы представляют из себя качественные пазлы с зачастую грамотно выставленными чекпоинтами..  Если же нет... ТО ЭТА СВОЛОЧЬ НАЧИНАЕТ ПРОСТО СТРАДАТЬ ♥♥♥♥♥♥. Порой, мне приходилось буквально продираться через каждый экран этапа в лучших традициях The Messenger и фейлить всё в самом конце, потому что дизайнеру захотелось поставить там ловушку, на которую ты никак не можешь среагировать вовремя, и идти проходить всё заново, зачастую потратив ещё N-ное кол-во попыток, чтобы иметь возможность хотя бы рассмотреть эту сраную ловушку. А, да: чекпоинт стоит в самом начале. В нескольких местах игра буквально заставляет сыграть скоростное соло на геймпаде, чтобы накрутить сложности. Тут эмоции могут варьироваться от осознания собственной крутости, до сожаления, что утроба матери разрабов не была похожа на тоннель с шипами на стенках.   Есть ещё пара моментов за которые хочется поругать:  1) нельзя настроить управление на геймпаде (даже ДМС 3 на второй плойке давал это сделать) 2) Когда герой стоит близко к стенке, второй прыжок будет отскоком от стены, а не дабл джампом 3) Если в прыжке сделать атаку и ноги гг окажутся над платформой, не факт, что он на эту платформу приземлится.  4) В испытаниях, за прохождение которых дают осколки ключа, реализовали, поход, самые хуёвые идеи разрабов. Они тупо представляют из себя битву на истощение: или тебя тупо заебёт проходить длиннющий коридор с препятствиями без передышки и права на ошибку, с отлётом на самое начало после каждого фэйла, или тебе повезёт и тебе хватит терпения и везения запомнить, как обходить все то говно, что девелоперы раскидали на твоём пути. Лично меня хватило на одно такое испытание.   ОБУЧЕНИЕ:  С обучением аще какая-то дичь твориться. То тебе расскажут и покажут, как каждую комбуху делать в игре, то забудут сказать, что в воздухе каждую специальную атаку можно прожать только один раз. Первый босс вроде считывается легко, а на арене используются вещи, с которыми ты уже успел столкнуться в данже перед боссом. Но второй босс - это какая-то ♥♥♥♥♥♥ из параш. Мало того, что тебе нужно считать её паттерны, пока она на заднем плане маячит, а твоё внимание занято врагами, так от её атак ещё и можно уворачиваться... Хотя они белые и фиолетовые... ХОТЯ ТАКОГО ЖЕ ЦВЕТА АТАКИ ТЕБЯ ДО ЭТОГО ВАНШОТАЛИ И ИГРА СООБЩАЛА, ЧТО ПЕРЕКАТ ПРОТИВ НИХ НЕ РАБОТАЕТ... Почему бляха муха, нельзя было этого босса показать раньше: запереть гг с ней в комнате, где её нельзя коцать, заставить игрока уворачиваться от её атак. А ещё лучше стригерить босса получить люлей от своей же собственной атаки, направив луч в зеркало или ещё что-нить?   ВЫВОД: надо было тестить. Как Valve тестили Portal. Каждый аспект. Каждую комнату. А так, получилось то, что получилось. Но всё равно - лучше чем у HK.

Amazing platformer and good hack-and-slash. A solo experience, but definitely better with 1-3 friends.  The platforming is challenging, but doable. It will frustrate you, but it feels fair. The puzzles can be very clever and are imaginative. If you know the predecessor, rest assured the puzzles are new: they added new mechanics that fit the game perfectly yet feel brand-new.  The fighting aspect is fun and offer a break between platforming. It can be a challenge if you play solo. Being with a friend makes it the much easier.  The humour and tone are nice, the aesthetics are breathtaking, and the music adds to the good ambience. Compared to the first game, the narrative is richer (if still absurd) and the jokes feel better thought-out. At a visual level, it is greatly improved.  Definitely worth buying and playing once, twice or more. As a plus, it is not one of those games that is impossible to finish at 100%. Stop reading. Go play it.

首先一句话简单评论，欢乐版空洞骑士 论战斗，实际上是不难的，比空洞还是要简单不少的，无非就是几种特殊技的灵活使用，考验对多种技能的搭配运用 但论跑酷，实在是太难了！前面提到的特殊技，实际上也是跑酷技能，但由于实在是太多了，算上，人和鸡两种形态一共九种特殊技，还有阴阳切换，爬墙，二段跳，钩锁，十几种跑酷技能，实际跑酷时，是需要短时间，在正确的节点释放出正确技能，说实话，个人认为，这个游戏的跑酷是能和蔚蓝一个档次的。蔚蓝属于是几个按键发挥到极致，这个游戏就是考验在诸多按键中混合使用的反应和编排，很容易就猪脑过载，手跟不上脑子，另外，使用手柄就更难受了，特别容易出现放错技能的情况。 剧情方面，很常规的反派抢走圣物，企图统治世界，主角拯救世界的剧情。但是这个游戏叙述方式是幽默的，中间页穿插了许多彩蛋，语言、图象页充满欢乐气息，梗也是非常多的。 画风和音乐方面，浓浓的墨西哥风，是对我胃口的。 综上所述，很难想象这是一个两个G的游戏。 个人没玩过一代，感觉直接玩二代没啥问题。 如果对这种平台跳跃战斗游戏感兴趣的话，毫无疑问是值得原价购入的游戏

Guacamelee! 2 to udana kontynuacja pierwszej części. Ponownie wcielamy się w Juana który u boku swojej rodziny prowadzi spokojne życie jednak pewnego dnia otrzymuje zadanie uratowania świata. Nasz bohater wskakuje w fatałaszki luchadora i stawia czoła niebezpieczeństwu. Każdy kto ogrywał jedynkę poczuje się tu jak w domu. Po raz kolejny lądujemy w niesamowitym świecie nawiązującym do meksykańskiej kultury i folkloru. Jeśli chodzi o rozgrywkę to nadal jest to platformówka urozmaicona rozbudowaną walką. Wracają dobrze znane ciosy ale tym razem możemy je rozwijać i ulepszać dzięki pięciu rożnym trenerom. Zmodyfikowana został również walka gdy przybieramy postać kurczaka. Nasz drobiowy bohater (jak to brzmi :D) opanowuje całkiem skuteczne ciosy. Na naszej drodze spotkamy starych dobrze znanych jak i nowych przeciwników. Warto jeszcze wspomnieć że ponownie stoczymy walki z świetnie zaprojektowanymi bossami. Podsumowując. Guacamelee! 2 to bardzo dobra gra zarówno dla osób które ograły pierwszą część jak i dla tych które poprzedniczki nie znają. Serdecznie polecam.  P.S. Ogrywane na Steam Decku na którym wszystko śmiga rewelacyjnie :)

El progreso es muy dinámico, cada poco tiempo te dan una habilidad nueva, es un ritmo de progresión que me recordó al segundo ori, y eso es bueno. I would like to give this game a neutral but ultimately I think it's more good than bad, the game looks great and for some people the gameplay will be cool.  For me, the problem ultimately lies in the fact that your inputs just feel a tad too slow.  As you progress you have to match your attacks to what color the enemies are outlined in and they will be different colors requiring different moves in the same arena.  The combat just isn't snappy enough for me, I'd say its more methodical in what you do, which is well and good but when I'm playing a metroidvania I prefer a faster one.  Also if this game just had a dash instead of a "dash attack" it would alleviate a lot of problems, since your movement is tied to combat moves.  The chicken is cool though.  If you're on the fence I would say wait for a sale and try it out, but you're not going to get movement like you would in Ori or Hollow Knight.  Going to finish it though.  6/10

I bought this game after how much I loved Guacamelee 1, and this game didn't disappoint! It has much larger maps than the original, a refined artstyle that's absolutely beautiful, more than twice as much enemy-types, and a story that's twice as good as the original's! It also notably adds something really awesome: In the first game, the chicken-transformation only existed as a mock-crouch ability that couldn't really do much until you get a bunch of golden medals in El Infierno, and even then it only got more platforming-abilities, rather than new attacks. But this game gives the chicken-transformation just that! In fact, the chicken can now fight nigh-on par with normal Juan, and has more-in-depth platforming moves! The chicken is also a lot more focused-on in this game, featuring its own dedicated platform-sections. The bosses are also stand out among one another even more than in the last game, and the characters are just as, if not more memorable! My only real criticism is that it only has 4 new moves, 3 of which are the chicken's. But still, this game is definitely a great sequel, and I highly recommend it if you liked the original.

与其说这是个“类恶魔城银河战士”的动作游戏，不如说是个跳平台的游戏更确切。而且跳平台部分手感还做得不好。 究其原因，太复杂了。一边跳一边还要切换人/鸡形态，还要切换阳间/阴间。完成一个场景的推进，往往要在短时间内按大量的不同的按键组合。如果蔚蓝为代表的平台跳跃为的是化繁为简：就一个跳跃一个冲刺。那这个就是化简为繁的代表：切换阴阳（来回按RT，因为有的机关在阴才能用，但同时阳的机关就不能用了，要瞬时切换才行），一会在空中按Y使用跳跃机关，一会使用上勾拳/冲刺拳（↑+B/→+B），一会切换鸡形态（LB），然后有时候需要切换鸡形态放一个招式后立刻切人放另一个招式。举个例子：起跳（A）立刻切换阴阳（RT因为起跳的跳台在阴间，然后帮助你跳跃的机关在阳间）然后在合适的位置抓钩跳（Y）然后二段跳（A）然后一个上勾拳（↑+B）距离差点来个冲刺拳(→+B)然后目标平台在阴间，所以最后要再按一下（RT），这个还算是比较简单的谜题 虽然勉强通了关（普通结局），但是过程一点都不愉快。当年是看在可以多人合作的“类恶魔城”游戏才买的，但是一直没人一起玩，所以也就放在库里吃灰。最近想起来干脆单机玩玩，结果就这鸟体验。不是我说，还没战斗方块剧场有意思………………

21.5个小时把地图舔干净获得100%地图收集率。这游戏的难度在我所有通关的银河城里是最高的。还有几个战斗相关的成就懒得做了。跳跳乐的确很难，挑战之地的15个房间有一两个死了上百次才过，要是五年前我肯定不用死这么多次，因此清晰地感觉到年龄增长带来的反应力衰退，打到后面过每一关基本都是靠背板，需要反应的地方基本都是靠命堆出来。但是作者贴心地把难点的复活位置设置得很近，而且地图没有任何随机性，因此就算是老年人经过足够的练习也是必然能过关的，而打通这些几乎是我能打过的难度上限的关卡带来的成就感也是巨大的，我觉得这就是Guacamelee! 2最大的优点。抛开蔚蓝，苦痛之路这种一看我就调头走人的游戏，这款浓郁南美风格的游戏通过合理的难度曲线让我乐意去挑战一些平常不太可能完成的关卡。

Guacamelee é uma franquia que desde que eu me dou por gente eu lembro dela estar presente na minha vida através de anúncios que eu via na tv falando sobre, o jogo original de 2013 é um jogo extremamente importante pro mercado indie, foi junto dele e de outros jogos como super meat boy,  the binding of isaac, shovel knight e limbo que jogos indie começaram a se tornar fíguras relevantes pra indústria de jogos, tudo isso antes desse tipo de produção realmente explodir não só em vendas mas em prestígio a partir da segunda metade da década passada, com títulos como stardew valley, hollow knight, celeste, dead cells e disco elysium, e mesmo que eu nunca tenha jogado o jogo original e só joguei o 2, deu pra ter uma noção do que deve ter feito o primeiro jogo ter se destacado tanto em 2013 quanto agora na minha jogatina quase uma década depois do lançamento do jogo original. Guacamelee! 2 tem uma direção de arte única e com uma personalidade absurda, desde os designs mais cartoonescos dos personagens até os fundos de tela lindamente decorados com tanto carinho, um senso de humor que é muito bem colocado e que me tirou umas boas risadas enquanto eu jogava, e uma progressão de combate e movimentação que todo metroidvania bom tem que ter que faz o jogo ficar cada vez mais flexível e inspirado com cada mecânica nova e como elas se comunicam com todas as outras apresentadas anteriormente e uma história que apesar de não ser muito complexa, é muito boa e o final me deixou muito feliz! Guacamelee 2 é simplesmente sensacional em todos os aspectos possíveis. Acho que a única reclamação que eu tenho é a dificuldade do jogo que depois de um certo ponto perde qualquer tipo de perigo e o jogo vira mais um god simulator do que um jogo de ação, o que nem é uma contradição quando tu vê a lore das máscaras, mas acho que isso também é em partes minha culpa já que eu fiquei tão sugado pelo jogo que eu quis pegar todos power up possíveis e tava praticamente todo upado na hora de chegar na área final do jogo, então leve isso em conta sobre oq eu acho da dificuldade. em resumo, guacamelee! 2 é um jogo incrível e que eu super recomendo pra qualquer um comprar, e se você acha o jogo caro é só esperar uma promoção, esse jogo vive na beirada dos 30 conto.

الجزء الثاني تفوّق على نفسه مقارنة بالأول، نفس المعايير العالية لكن بأسلوب أنضج وأكشن أكثر متعة 💥🐔، المطورين حافظوا على هوية اللعبة وأضافوا عليها بشكل عبقري، سواء من ناحية التبديل بين العوالم أو المهارات الجديدة، حتى حس الفكاهة رجع وبقوة، اللعبة بشكل عام ممتعة لأقصى درجة وتستاهل وقتك من البداية للنهاية 👊🔥.  The second part really surpassed itself compared to the first, keeping the same high standards but with a more refined style and even more enjoyable action 💥🐔. The developers stayed true to the game’s identity while adding clever upgrades like world switching and new abilities. Even the humor came back stronger. Overall, it’s a super fun experience that’s worth playing from start to finish 👊🔥.

There are some familiar feelings we all experience in games. One of them is: "Wow, that was so much fun, I want to do that again sometime!" , and that was entire first Guacamelee! for me. In the second game, this feeling appears in many places too, but now there are too many moments of type "Good god, what a relief that this is behind me, I don't want to ever repeat this level"  + solid and fun combat is back, feels amazing as ever! + nice music (but far from being as epic as in the first game) + plenty of crazy sense of humor  - too many timed challenges and timed enemies, the game feels more stressful now - much darker backgrounds and colour palette. there are too many dark metroidvanias already and the first game was so cool because it was colourful and refreshing! - too many jokes and references to other games and media, also too many moments where the game is "self aware" and is commenting itself. I would prefer more immersion and for the game to stand solely on its own world and setting - Tostada having a cape now with every costume.. why? The sequel to the amazing Guacamelee!, Guacamelee! 2 mostly just brings more of the same, with some new bells and whistles. However, I also feel it loses some of its identity. At its core, its still a great metroidvania with a Mexican setting, although it takes place in a different timeline. And I think that was a misstep. The graphics are improved, most notably the lighting and reflections, however the new zones in this darker timeline mostly look the same to me. They just feel more generic. Not only that, but I also thought the soundtrack to be slightly worse overall. The story picks up from the first game ending, and gives a nice excuse for losing your powers. However, I wasn’t immediately hooked like I was in the first game. It took me around 1 hour to really start enjoying the game. Gameplay is still where the game really shines, and it’s even better this time. There’re more abilities and variety in both platforming and combat. For example, the chicken form can now fight competently. There’re more enemies and new obstacles as well. The game is also a bit longer, so no complains there. Not only that, it’s also a bit harder. You’ll especially notice that in some optional sections, if you’re hunting for 100%. It’s not a hardcore platformer, but some sections get a bit close to that, especially the final chicken gauntlet. That makes 100% less enjoyable, and less of a recommendation. But it’s still doable and rewarding. What I didn’t enjoy at all was the inclusion of DLC, one only offering 3 costumes and the other offering challenge rooms similar to the Inferno ones in the first game. They should’ve just been included in the base game, or free updates. Also, no more Steam Workshop. To me, Guacamelee! 2 takes one step forward in gameplay and one step back in the overall setting. It’s more of the same, but not in a bad way. Another easy recommendation, although I recommend you play this after the first game.

This is a great follow up to the original. The combat retains its satisfying feel. The movement mechanics are interesting, using the combat moves as a central part of the movement. There are some fun platforming challenges throughout. It's just a really enjoyable gameplay experience that flies by.  The co-op option was extremely fun. I was able to play the whole thing with my brother and we had an awesome time. Some of the combat challenges were made much easier (perhaps too easy) with 2 of us, as they seemed to be tuned to single-player without any scaling factors for co-op. This was a small detail, and with 2 of us trying to find time to get together to play, it was helpful to have fast-paced progress without getting hung up on some more grind-y combat challenges.  This game felt more obviously linear in its progression, and you collected power ups very quickly. These power ups were also very similar to the first game. This isn't necessarily a bad thing, as the first game was a lot of fun, but it doesn't do much to revolutionize the first game. There's one obvious exception, but I won't ruin that.  I highly recommend this game for a fun, fast-paced, 2-D game.

The key to enjoying this game is to not have played the first one right before it.  Guacamelee 2 feels like a remix of Guacamelee 1, which is a good thing, but it doesn't evolve the gameplay enough to feel different.  So as I said, the key is to not have the first game fresh in your mind, so you can enjoy the core gameplay loop without feeling like "I just did all this!"

I feel like I have a good view of the game as I just played it right after I finished the first one. Here is my analysis:  Gameplay: It's more of the same which is good. The fighting mechanics themselves have not changed nor are there any additional powers as a Luchador. Even though the fighting mechanics haven't changed, they added a few abilities and an upgrading system. The upgrading system adds much more to the game as well as some new side characters to join the fun. You can also find a giant improvement as a chicken. Playing as a chicken to fight skeletons has never felt more satisfying and possible in this adventure. Lucha battles are arguably the same, however, boss battles are visually more entertaining and intense.  There are some setbacks in the second game of this installment. The main issue the majority would agree upon is the vastly added RED spikes to the puzzles in the game. If you played the first game you already understand, to explain the issue, it essentially is a one-hit KO with a screen transition to start over again from the start of the puzzle rather than GREEN spikes which start you from the last solid standing spot with no screen transition. The screen transition though sounds harmless, becomes frustrating as many of these puzzles are trial and error focused. These small additional seconds make it feel like a slow drag to push forward and are not needed to improve the difficulty of the game. The RED spikes should be used as a pinch of salt rather than an engulfing of it.   Story: There are setbacks and strong strides forward. The largest issue with the plot of the story is that it is extremely silly. For many, that will not be an issue. However, coming from the serious plot of the first game, the plot seems out of place. All that being said, the writing has improved immensely. The comedy really stands out throughout the game, and I myself had a lot of laughs and enjoyment from the jokes. It doesn't feel like they needed a funny plot as the writing takes care and owns the comical relief in the game. It easily could have had a serious plot as the first installment, with this game's comedic writing. Not to mention, there is an additional side story in this game as a chicken, which is hilarious and a much more fitted area for comical relief than the main plot. Because of this game, I theorize that a well-written story with a weak plot is better than a weak plot with bad writing.   Visuals: I will not be talking much about the visuals in this game. I will state, it carries the charm and color of the first installment. There are small fine details that have been added. Example: Reflections in the green water. The enemies have been upgraded with a new coat of bone which would come down to the opinion of whether they look better or not, that being said, I believe the designs are largely improved. Locations and areas are still bright and charming but I find them not as memorable as they were in the first game because there were fewer reasons to revisit said locations during gameplay.   Price: It's worth it on sale if you don't have the first game, and worth full price if you own the first game. It's best to play the first installment (but not needed) before purchasing the second game due to the first being easier, making it a comfortable transition from one installment into the other rather than vice versa.  Conclusion: This game is a novel in the indie Metroidvania genre. This game isn't exactly a giant improvement from the first but rather a more finely tuned version of the first installment. It's just as fun and enjoyable as the first installment with many more laughs and chicken action. As the first game, this makes for a great gift for people on Cinco De Mayo.   Score: 8/10

I enjoyed the first Guacamelee, and this one delivers much of the same -- a humorous metroidvania with various special moves which you unlock to break various bricks. There were a lot of references and memes in the game, and I certainly found myself laughing out loud multiple times, which is always a big plus for me.  Like the first game, combat isn't too difficult, especially once you've acquired an arsenal of special moves and power-ups. The platforming sections, on the other hand, felt like they did get more difficult compared to the first game. Many areas have instant death walls/floors/ceilings of spikes/lava/void, with a lot of split-second timing needed to jump between platforms while swapping between the world of the dead and world of the living. In fact, I would say the difficulty was slightly higher than I prefer.  And yet, I beat the game in a reasonable amount of time anyway without too much frustration, because the high difficulty is very kind in two ways:  1) The most difficult content is all optional, which means I could simply nope out of the impossible mid-air fight over the lava floor, or the endless jump up dancing between walls of spikes.  2) When the game does have instant-death walls in a difficult section, dying generally brings you back *INSTANTLY* to the beginning of the room. Not 7 rooms back, not 30 seconds to reload, but instantly. So while in other games I might get very dispirited and annoyed having to try a difficult platforming section 5 times and taking a long time to get back to the tricky part each time, here there were rooms which took me well over a dozen tries but still only took me a few minutes.  Overall, a very fun (and funny!) and satisfying gaming experience. Would recommend to anyone who doesn't hate difficult platforming -- and if you do normally hate reloading to try difficult platforming, you might like it anyway, since it lets you iterate really fast. I wish more games would do that. Regardless, one of the funniest games I've played in years, and very glad to have done so.

Guacamelee 2 is one of those games that I really wish was just more stylistically appealing to me because ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥, the core gameplay here is genuinely pretty awesome for the most part! If you've played the first game in the series then you'll basically get the exact same experience here, with this more being an exercise in iteration rather than transformation, which is all in all totally fine in this case when so much has been refined and expanded upon to this high degree, especially with more effectively understanding how to balance out the 2 biggest elements of its loop, the platforming and combat. In general I feel like this leans far more heavily into the platforming elements of its experience after the first hour or so, with the dungeons you explore feeling more akin to long, linear stretches of nonstop obstacles that force you to use the vast majority of your surprisingly varied moveset. While the player will still have multiple instances of being trapped in confined spaces and having to fight their way out, even these often end up being more interesting due to the creative setups that still make you use your moves for their mobility benefits on top of damage outputs.  The way that each move you get doubles both as a mobility tool and a combat one is a huge draw for me and helps make each new upgrade you come across feel rather transformative and keeps things interesting by the way these keep expanding the possibilities of what you can get done in all facets of the game. By the end of it all, you'll have so many different methods of getting around that it borders on overwhelming without ever quite crossing that line, turning every obstacle into something that you'll need to actively have a moment dedicated to just thinking how you can chain everything together to reach where you want to. This works excellently to keeping up a sense of flow, where even the downtime between big moments still feels deeply involved and satisfying. I also feel that the devs clearly had the most fun devising this aspect of the game, which can be seen through the way that there are so many little secret areas that get technical enough to straight up delve into precision platformer territory.  These little side challenges ended up feeling more exciting and rewarding to discover than the actual rewards the game gives out to you as well, because just like in the first, collecting the hidden chests doesn't really feel like anything too special thanks to how easy it mostly feels even without them, since the real challenge this offers comes almost exclusively from tricky platforming sections. The way that this game goes for the angle of being that bit bigger and more expansive also ends up being a bit of a negative when considering the collecting and exploring angle that metroidvanias push. The amount of time to return to quite a few locations later in order to pick up stray treasure chests you might've missed is an absolute pain, often getting you to replay sizeable chunks of a linear level all over again to reach them, just for it to often be, as previously stated, nothing that you actually care about much unless you're just going for full completion. Despite these smaller complaints, the gameplay is still very, very solid, with the combat being tuned in a lot of respects to feel more natural and flowing than the first without really changing anything about its identity, and moving around is still a genuinely wonderful time, it's just a shame then that I find the game really obnoxious in other regards.  The writing here sucks, maybe even more than the first game which I already thought was at times, painful to sit through. The game brings in a multiverse plot near the start, already something I find to be pretty immediately repellent, but then instead of using this for literally anything creative at all, it's squandered by just making every other dimension you travel to a direct reference to some other video game. Basically every time you go through one of these dimensional portals you just know that you'll have to brace yourself to an agonisingly hollow attempt at making you laugh by virtue of "haha, I recognise that game!!!" and while the first time it's y'know, whatever, by the 4th or 5th it becomes infuriating. This is especially prominent in the "dankest dimension" which basically just reuses all the ♥♥♥♥♥♥ meme images that were plastered all over the first game while also having a bunch of NPCs mock people who played the game for saying that these references were awful even when released. This self-awareness ends up making all of this even more frustrating as well, because it ends up transforming it all from dumb but earnest meme references into something that feels incredibly petty without being any funnier the 2nd time. It also makes all the other references shoddily plastered all over the place feel just as annoying, the only different being that instead of seeing the "me gusta" face, you're just seeing a giant cutout of the Frog Fractions frog, just as lazily implemented. The sense of artificiality still shines through as well, as there's still absolutely nothing to interestingly frame the uses for your different abilities. Blocks that only are destroyed when hit by a certain special attack still feel just as bland as ever, literally just being an out of place, brightly coloured block, same with the enemy forcefields still just being "match the colour". It's functional but certainly contributes to the game having this certain soulless vibe in its presentation that unfortunately goes against the genuinely expressive artstyle that this otherwise has.  If Guacamelee in general could just present itself better and not go so heavily into painful "haha so quirky!" humour then I could see it being a personal favourite in the genre, but with the way both this and the first game end up playing out, I'll just say that I'm glad the actual gameplay experience is strong enough to make me want to push through the garbage aspects at play. Definitely the sort of game that I'd recommend in general, but especially if you're a bit less allergic to rapid fire referencing as I am, there's a lot of cool stuff to be seen.  7/10

Juan Aguacate was the only luchador who could beat Thanos.   This game really makes you FEEL like a Luchador.  Guacamelee 2 is one of my favourite platformers. The main character and his struggles remind me to the days I would wrestle my wife’s boyfriend for the right to spend a night with my wife. I never won a match, but the memories we made were unforgettable.  Guac 2 has a toned down exploration but stellar, creative platforming throughout with much better pacing than the first. The chicken ability has its own subset of abilities, both in and out combat. The bosses were pretty good though short. The combat also benefits from having a few more moves and much greater enemy variety.  I think it's better overall than the first, but lacks the inherent freshness of the original. The art and music are a bit richer. The gameplay evolves faster and there's a couple new bells and whistles. It's a couple hours longer. And the camera is pulled back a bit more which makes 4-player co-op feel less crowded/cluttered. The broad strokes of the story is a little weaker than the original, but still charming.  It's kind of a retread of the first game. It has a few quirks I don't like (e.g. being unable to turn around mid-combo) and the improvements aren't very significant (I don't remember a single new song). I think it's better than the first, but kind of like how a remake is better than the original.  Definitely worth picking up, specially on a sale.  8/10

Decent platformer/metroidvania. Combat is very simple and easy, but still allows you to combo enemies if you want to bother. Most of the platforming sections are pretty easy, but there's some actual tough ones if you bother to look for the secret areas. Some annoyances I had is that there's a mechanic where you press a button on a floating eagle to launch yourself in a direction, but it's very finnicky in some areas and fails to launch you so you just instantly die and have to specifically angle yourself in a way to not get ♥♥♥♥♥♥ over by certain eagle launchers. Also grabbing enemies in the chicken form is wonky. There's also a really cringy area that's devoted to the devs "clapping back" at the people that didn't like the various memes in the first game and it just comes off as extremely petty.
Always good fun. Ez enough to run through. Culturally solid.  Punch gawk bawk

Guacamelee! 2 is a game about joys of Mexican cuisine. Or possibly, an awesome game in the same vein as Metroid. It is funny, challenging in a good way, and beautiful. While some of the platforming sections may cause sore thumbs, they are expertly designed challenges. And because of the precise controls and the ability to retry instantly, the frustration never sets in. The battle system is fun, and it has a lot of hidden depth in the form of combos, juggling etc. other fighting game mechanics. The game is not perfect though. I would say that the fast travel points are located a bit too far from each other, killing at least my desire to go back to previous locations in order to collect missed collectibles.  Can't say that I had many bad moments with Guacamelee! 2. If I had played longer than 1 hour sessions, that probably would have left me a sourer impression. Because of the pulverized thumbs. But in short sessions, it is absolutely worth the full price. 5/5

Guacamelee! 2 — самая мексиканская игра в мире, пусть и создана она канадцами. Это очевидно уже из ее названия: что в России клюква, то в Мексике — знаменитый соус гуакамоле (к которому добавили лишь слово melee — то есть ближний бой).  https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2885799474  Динамичный, веселый местами тяжелый платформер с пасхалками и большим кол-во секретов, большим количеством ироничных шуток и забавных персонажей, накидали новых противников, научили Хуана дополнительным приёмам и добавили непростых испытаний.  Графика тоже изменилась, что легко можно заметить при сравнении сиквела с предшественницей, — окружение стало детализированнее, а освещение радует глаз в каждой залитой солнцем локации.  Атмосферу прекрасно поддерживают чарующие музыкальные мотивы. Труба, гитара, маракасы! Мексика, друзья.  Как и всё, что происходит на экране, не заставит вас заскучать.  Как по мне, достойное продолжение первой части. У меня не было возможности поиграть по сети с друзьями, но если у вас будет такая возможность - обязательно попробуйте. Но местами "секреты" превратились в настоящий ад, где умираешь сотню раз, сотню раз повторяешь одно и тоже, надеясь, что тебе повезет.  Части Золотого ключа - это вообще нечто невозможное. Надоедает и иногда реально бесит проходить десятки раз одно и тоже.  https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2883394983  [h1]Плюсы:[/h1] + Такой же отличный стиль, как и в первой части, и по-прежнему хороший юмор;  + Боевая система обогатилась благодаря новым приёмам и противникам; + Интересное повествование сюжета; + За курицу играть стало гораздо интереснее, да и пользы от неё больше;  + Задорная музыка и похорошевшая графика; + Комнаты-головоломки всё так же увлекательны; + Наличие дополнительного контента; + Ко-оп на четверых.  [h1]Плюс / Минус:[/h1] +/- "секреты" превратились в настоящий ад, где умираешь сотню раз.  [h1]Минус:[/h1] - Нет онлайнового ко-опа.  https://steamcommunity.com/sharedfiles/filedetails/?id=2884208650

[i]Guacamelee! 2[/i] is a top-shelf Metroidvania. It is better than it's already excellent predecessor and comes pretty close to [i]Hollow Knight[/i] in my list of best Metroidvanias--[i]HK[/i] being the undefeated champion since its release. Here's why [i]G2[/i] is so amazing:  [list] [*]Varied, challenging, inventive, skill-based combat from beginning to end [*]TONS of secrets to find. Almost all of these are of the attribute upgrade variety but there is some visual storytelling involved too. There is also a very fun, really funny, game-spanning uber mystery to uncover that is as fun to solve as it is sometimes infuriating (a personal criterion for a game being among the "best" Metroidvanias). [*]The writing is perfect: It's stupid. It's funny. It short. I almost fell out of my chair laughing at multiple characters, most of them chickens. You'll understand. [*]Did I mention the game is ridiculously good-looking? [*]Every music track is a banger. There's no leitmotifs in this game but it's not exactly [i]Chrono Trigger[/i] heavy, ya dig? It's a fun game with good tunes. [/list]  If you look for something to dislike I'm sure you will find something (a lot of the later out-of-the-way challenge rooms are real kaizo nightmares and the memes you sometimes find on walls are pretty meh...if that's a deal-breaker for you). What [i]Guacamelee! 2[/i] lacks in dread and existential world-building mysteries it makes up for with style, fun-factor and humor.  Players who played the original should know that the combat challenges are mostly easier this time around. There is no melee challenge as awful as the Infierno Tower in [i]Guacamelee![/i] this time around. Moreover, you can unlock bonuses to permit up to 3 hits before breaking a combo. There are several quality-of-life improvements I won't mention here that can make challenging fights a cakewalk (not to say you won't die though) and the hardest fights survivable. The non-melee challenges, however, are much harder. It's like the developers played [i]Rayman Legends[/i] and said, "Let's do that, but frame-perfect." To be clear: This is a HARD GAME. You will die a lot no matter how many perks you unlock. If you try to get the best ending, a good chunk of your play time will be do-overs.  I would also like to mention that Player 1 can change their appearance to some of the characters from the first game immediately in single player mode. I played the whole game as X'tabay and strongly recommend it. She is a badass and looks damn good kicking it! I love the game. It is really fun in co-op but unfortunately that's where the main drawbacks are: - Moving between the screens  is often getting annoying when one of the players falls or moves to a different section and the game automatically moves the rest of the players. 说说个人感受，经历熔炉后完全断了我买DLC的念头，然后买这游戏最好不要只过剧情，最好做全收集。 说说优点： 1.地图真的大，虽然元素单一，但没那么单调； 2.大部分关卡设计都很不错，过关方法很明显，各有特点和挑战性，除了其中一小部分真的是折磨人和不当人。 3.打击感也不错，一个偏向平台类型的游戏，属实可以； 4.动作设计和空间反转真的不错，反正我脑子有时候是转不过来的，不理清思绪根本就过不了关（我菜我的问题），但设计的真好。 再说说缺点吧： 1.地图太大但传送点太少，以致于为了做全地图成就，跑得半死； 2.悬浮手感是真的差，纯纯的恶心人；

Actualmente el juego lo tengo en "recomendado" debido a que es un juego que funciona perfectamente y puedes jugarlo sin problemas si te gustan los metroidvania indies, pero tengo que reconocer que a mi NO me gustó. Es imposible no compararlo con un Ori o Hollow Knight en cuanto a mecánicas o jugabilidad (tienen un precio similar y todos parten de estudios pequeños)... Y al compararlos Guacamelee es sin duda el peor de ellos con mucha diferencia. Hazte con él sin ningún tipo de expectativas y es posible que lo disfrutes relativamente.  Pro - Ligeros toques de humor. - Cooperativo para 4 jugadores toda la campaña.  Contra - Control muy impreciso y que estropea enormemente la experiencia de juego. - El mapa invita poco a la exploración y esta poco inspirado. - El juego es muy exigente en términos de plataformeo para lo pobres que son los controles.  6/10

93/100 Afterthoughts after several months:  Guacamelee has got to be one of the best games ever made. Even after months after playing the memories come back. The music track still memorable, the gameplay mechanics immortal! I will be playing it again soon.   ______  Review after completing the game:  This is one of the most frustrating and yet satisfying game I have ever played.   - It has got those mad levels that one cannot think to complete and just 10 minutes of smashing your head in frustration on the table and you complete it. - Do you want to exercise your fingers? Play this game on the keyboard and and then you can lift heavyweights with your index and middle finger. - Do you think your brain-eye-hand coordination needs improvement? Play this game and brain, eye and hand will get fused together - Do you want to learn a new language to improve brain capacity, play this game, and you fingers would have learnt 2.  A controller is recommended to play this game, but I think it should be enjoyed on a keyboard. I think the hard difficulty on keyboard is the charm of it.  I cannot praise Guacamelee 1 and 2 enough.  One of the best games ever made, period.

Guacamelee 2 = Guacamelee 1: Super Sarcastic Sadistic Version  + Cool Graphics (Appeared to be the same as G1) + Good Humor/Sarcasm ( Better than G1)  + Fluid movement (Same as G1) + End game was fun and challenging  +- Much more Precision Platforming than G1 +- Didn't feel like a Metroidvania until the end game   - Overly Repetitive Enemies - Unnecessary Combos - Not enough Fast Travel Stations - Eagle Boosts   I can't explain it, as this game is basically Guacamelee 1 with a Gigantic new map and a few new skills... But I liked it better than G1.  The sarcasm and humor was great.  I found it a little hypocritical that they knocked other games with loot boxes, but then they hid half the of the games achievements behind two paid DLCs.  The fighting was really the easy part.  The platforming was tough in MANY places.  In particular the areas that relied on Eagle Boosts.  If you have played Ori, they have a similar idea, but Ori's use of them was much easier.  You jump towards it, and an arrow appears that you can change to go the direction you want.  In G2, the Eagle Boosts work in direct opposite relation to your position to it.  This made using them very challenging and cuss worthy (Mofos!).  Again, this game is tough... tougher than G1.  However, most places have fair/close check points.  Fast Travel Stations seemed to be an after thought.  Yes they are in the game but no, they are not very valuable most of the time because you end up having to traverse over the entire map to get somewhere.

I am still playing the game, and just wanted to share my thoughts on the game so far. This will be updated down the road.  The pros:  A Fun new gang of villains. The combat still is quite solid, simple, fun and responsive. The visuals are great, if a little busy at times. The music is spetacular and very unique as far as games are concerned. Boss fights are crazy fun and creative (I am particularly a big fan of the Muñeco fight).  Cons:  The worst thing about this game is the instant kill nonsense. It is everywhere. The first game was challenging, and this one tries to be even harder, but in the most obnoxious ways possible. I love me some platforming, challenging platforming, but the challenge here often feels like playing the "True Ending" route of Cave Story, but that section of Cave Story was added later in the development of a game developed by one person. Guacamelee 2 lacks that excuse.   Now, Guacamelee doesn't send you back to the beginning of a level/dungeon when you die, thankfully. It is a little bit like Celeste - you die and quickly respawn, only less refined, because the real insta-death hazards sometimes send you back a few screens away, and some of the challenging sections are VERY obnoxious. I feel like Guacamelee does a fun job at mixing up good platforming, beat em up combat and some fun bullet dodging sections, but sometimes platforming, melee combat AND bullet hell all at the same time is too much.  Imagine you are playing Celeste, trying to grab that pesky strawberry, and every time you die, the game sends you back two, three screens. At first it doesn't bother you, but after five, ten times? It gets old. I think if they removed the insta-kill nonsense, the game would suddenly go up a few degrees in quality.   Other than that, the humour still heavily relies on references and memes. It wasn't particular interesting the first time, and it falls even flatter to me this time around. I suppose this is merely a matter of taste, however.   Also, you still have the option to play as Tostada, and other characters, but just like in Guacamelee, the story doesn't really reflect that in any way or shape. It is still is Juan's story, and that is perfectly fine, it just feels like playing as other characters is a feature just because that is the norm for beat em ups.    So, other than a few very annoying annoyances, it is a solid game so far. I wonder if my thoughts will change after I finish it. For reference, the original Guacamelee was one of the few games I ever 100% (in terms of achievements), so I am pretty big fan of the game.

[h1]The Good[/h1] +Combat fluidity feels better than the first game. Chaining special attacks feels snappier +Introduction of Pollo combat is a good addition. Movement with it feels great +Eagle-jumping is really fun +Forced realworld/deadworld platform segments are challenging and fun +Better graphics. Improved lighting and effects +Music is great, on par with first game +Dumb but fun in-game minigames referencing. Funny writing +Some good new enemy designs +Muñeco's boss theme is great! +Key-collecting stages are fun as fu¢k; a tough challenge as a proof of movement/ability mastery is (almost) always a good time   [h1]The Bad[/h1] -Not that many new special attacks (pollo shot, one new grab, ...?) -There are not many improvements to the combat overall. No significant changes over the 3-hit basic combo or special attacks -Challenges are DLC? -Costumes are DLC? -Salvador [spoiler]was hyped up so much in the story, but didn't deliver [/spoiler] -Muñeco's boss theme is [i][b]the[/b][/i] only boss theme? -There are way too many one-shot environmental dangers. It's balanced that they both kill enemies [i]and[/i] you, but it's boring either way -Parts of the game blocked by NPCs telling you 'You should go here first!', instead of using actual gameplay mechanics is lazy design -Power-ups are too good. They improve your damage-dealing capabilities way too much, but the game doesn't offer challenges to match -Late game, the 3 Hit combo is practically useless besides breaking its respective shield. Everything else does way more damage and offers better crowd control    [h1]Bottom line[/h1] Doesn't feel like a true successor, instead feels like Guacamelee 1.5. Buy only on a sale  [h2]The second bottom line[/h2] Screw you drinkbox, the non-vidya memes in the first games were out of place Dedicating a whole section just to 'dab' on people who didn't like them makes you look butthurt and lame

4th time completing this game (Xbox, Playstation, Switch, now Steam). Several references to other games, good humor, and seems to show great love and respect to Mexican culture.  One of those special Metrovania's that should be played by anyone that likes platformers and great combat. I will admit, there are a few combat and platforming challenges tied to the good ending that may take some people a good while to complete, but it's all definitely doable. Also, I want to note how much of a good touch the color-coded enemy shields can be. It's subtle, but adds that extra layer of challenge which is much appreciated.

I like its art and the music, but I find the controls infuriating, and some save points further than they should be in a game that takes its platforming so dead serious. Backtracking through challenging areas just because you missed one exit on the map makes me want to smash the nearest controller. If you accidentally miss your shortcut, you can also brace yourself for some much-disliked backtracking. Do not give me areas where you need less-than-a-second react time, and then spread my save points too far from the last checkpoint, and then make your controls less than pitch-perfect. It's a recipe for disaster, and no amount of crafty art and pretty music can save the game play.

[b]Podes pelear como un pollo hermano COMO UN POYO![/b]  [url=https://store.steampowered.com/curator/36430761-KurareMHM/][quote]Para más recomendaciones hace click wachin[/quote][/url]  El juego mejora todo lo del 1, algo malo de esto también es que el juego esta pensado para gente que se termino el primer titulo y si entra alguien a jugar que no domine las mecánicas del juego (como si lo hará alguien que termino el 1) puede que se le haga bastante difícil, pero no imposible obvio. Los creadores se contuvieron en usar muy seguido a sus enemigos más molestos  (como los de las boleadoras o las tortuguitas con pinchos).  Hay 3 grandes mejoras que me encantaron con respecto al primer juego  1- El modo pollo ya no es solo para pasar por lugares chiquitos, ahora es un estilo de combate con sus movimientos propios y hasta combos propios (y hasta tiene importancia narrativa)   2- Los enemigos y jefes ahora si son un peligro. En el 1 aún en dificil eran solo sacos de boxeo hasta los jefes, pero ahora con los enemigos y jefes si te pueden matar. Más si usas el Codigo Konami y desbloquear "Dificil" (recomendado si terminaste el 1)  3- Las ramas de habilidades, eso de no tener que ir a la tienda cada vez que quieras algo agradezco mucho.   Luego hay muchas mejoras de animaciones, más detalles, etc. Las referencias, memes y el humor esta intacto (de esto no diré detales por que arruinaría los chistes del juego xD)  En fin un gran metroidvania que envejece muy bien por su originalidad, creo que consiguió ese equilibrio de dificultad entre combate, jefes y plataformeo que buscaban. Merece la verdad ser más conocido el juego por que derrocha carisma y calidad honestamente.

[h1]TL;DR[/h1] Drinkbox follows up their seminal [i]Guacamelee[/i] with another fantastic entry into the series. The adventure is bigger, there's more to do, and there's more memes than ever.  [h1]Sentinels of the Mexiverse[/h1] For those of us who have played the first game, [i]Guacamelee 2[/i] takes the first game and adds a fair bit of quality-of-life improvements. A lot of the familiar mechanics are still here but with some good improvements. Combat is actually a little easier this time around, as there are fewer spammy enemies as there were in the first one, so you're less likely to get stun locked by a crowd. Those flying bombs that 1-shot you also aren't as prevalent. Additionally, the bosses aren't as much of a damage sponge as they originally were; instead. This isn't to say the fights aren't challenging, they just feel more "fair" than the first one.  The platforming has also gotten some polish here. Mandatory sections of the map have been made slightly easier while the optional collectibles hide behind much harder challenges. Most prominently, a "grappling hook" ability was added into your roster of movement options and with it came a lot of fun new platforming challenges that they couldn't reasonably do in the first game.  There are a two weaker points to note though. First, the storyline of this game is a bit weaker; the big baddie and in particular his crew of henchmen don't really [b]feel[/b] like they're that motivated. They're just sorta there, without any real reason to be present. The final boss fight was also a bit of a let-down. The second weak point is that the co-op is simply not that good. The core gameplay doesn't lend itself to co-op well in many sections, particularly the platforming, which is a shame cause the combat is actually quite fun in co-op. If you're just planning on playing this solo, then obviously the co-op is not a concern at all. I felt a bit mixed on the Hard Mode. Simply scaling enemy damage up felt like a cheap way of implementing difficultly, but changing up the platforming puzzles for some areas was a nice touch.  [h1]Breakdown[/h1] [b]Pros[/b] [olist]     [*][b]Flair for Days:[/b] The artwork, animation, and visual style remains fantastic.      [*][b]Ludomatic Mexiverse:[/b] Some of most memorable and interesting bits of the game were the portals to the throwback and references areas. I wished there were more of these, as most of the sections were a blast. [/olist] [b]Cons[/b] [olist]     [*][b]Knife Bipod:[/b] [i]Guacamelee 2[/i] has a skill tree of sorts where players can upgrade their abilities. However, the skill tree itself is quite underwhelming, with only a couple being actual skills while most of the upgrades are simply damage upgrades. All-in-all, it felt underbaked.     [*][b]Chicken Little:[/b] The good ending of the game is locked behind 6 chicken mini-dungeons. These 6 dungeons also represent the 6 toughest sections of the game (outside of the Challenge Arenas). 4 of these dungeons were fine and quite fun, the remaining 2 were quite tedious. Some of these Chicken puzzles could have definitely been toned down a bit without losing any of the fun-factor. [/olist]  [b]Score[/b]: 8/10

This is a rare review for me because I got so bored I didn't even want to finish the game. It's still gonna get a positive review since I believe this isn't a bad game. Let's explore the ups and downs of the game (+, +/-, -, ?):  + The game looks great. + The controls are mostly responsive and fine. + I love the humor and the puns. Freaking love those billboards :D +/- The OST is quite generic for the theme, but it fits the game nicely. +/- When it comes to the story, this sequel isn't doing anything better than the original. This game's story has a "Jason X" feel to it, and not in the good way. However, it's not outright bad. - While the story isn't bad, nothing really matters in the game. I felt that there's nothing on the line. This is because everything happens so fast. In a way, this is a common "speed run game" problem. Fast action over story never really works for me. It might for you. - I stopped many times to ponder why I'm playing the game because I was trying to force myself to like the game. I didn't. ? It's almost a copy-paste experience with nothing improved over the original. ? Is this just a bad time to play a nothing happening platformer? Maybe, and if so, I'll update my review.  Guacamelee 2 is a perfect nothing of a video game. It's an okay platformer with nothing special to it, outside the art style. If you're up for a platformer with an unique art style, this might be the game for you. But, it's no different from the first one. It may actually be worse. If I ever finish the game, I'll update this review. But for now, it's right in the middle my scale to me, 5/10.

这种东西我没法推荐给其他玩家。  按理说，主角是摔跤手，图的就是一个紧张刺激的打斗，可以使用凶猛的拳术与腿法，各种来路不明的桌椅板凳道具，还有堪比体操队操练现场的花式终结技，再不济，也能路见不平一声吼，吓退雄狮九千万，这才是玩家想要追求的摔跤手游戏。  本作则反其道而行之，每个地区的敌人打完一次就不再刷新，大部分敌人还是在特定地形下摆开有利阵型，有组织地对抗主角，这倒是没什么，关键是赖招太多了，敌人经常使用各种必中技、飞行道具、大范围攻击和霸体来耍赖，打的不痛快不说，主角的操作也超级笨重，按键反应极慢，多余动作极多，根本没法正常玩耍，相比敌人身轻如燕来去如风的走位，打赢全靠硬拼，而且经常是主角挨揍，这让人很难受。  更可气的地方是，本作有许多翻越难度极高的地形，对起落点和时机的把握要求特别高，一个摔跤手游戏搞波斯猴子那一套干嘛？更何况主角的操作十分笨重，这就导致每到一个考验反应的区域时都会让人连声叹息，怎么还来呀！都走过一次复杂地形了，怎么还有呀！主角长这一身肌肉是来快意恩仇打小怪兽的，不是让你耍猴玩的！  总而言之，不行，真的不行，可惜了这创意。

Por sí solo: Entretenido, muy bello, bien diseñado en términos de niveles y mecánicas, divertido, buena música. Respecto del primero: Me pareció más sencillo y más corto que el primero, pero a la vez más justo en su jugabilidad y menos frustrante. Me reí considerablemente más con esta secuela y si bien ambos juegos tienen una gran banda sonora, la música de este me gustó más (el tema de Los Manglares es espectacular). [spoiler]La diferencia entre el buen y el mal final es muy parecida a la del primer juego, dado que en cualquier caso el final es alegre y tierno, pero el mal final deja un toque de amargura.[/spoiler] Lo único que me desagradó fue el hecho de incluir algunas micro-transacciones, no presentes en el primero. Puntos extra por darle más protagonismo a la habilidad de transformarse en pollo.  Lo compré en oferta y lo valió bastante, aunque no sé si lo compraría a precio completo. De todas maneras, 8/10, recomendado.

Disclaimer: Beat the game once with a bad ending. I didn't get the good ending because I already got all the fun out of this game. Trying to get the good ending would make me have a worse opinion of this good game. The special challenges are too hard and they are really not for me, so I quit.  It's Guacamelee! 1 with a bigger budget. It's got platforming and stuff down, but its visual clarity is very bad as:  1. Sometimes enemies get cluttered in the screen and you can't find Juan out of all the mess. 2. When you don't have enough special bar, your character turns red. This is a helpful indicator, but the background uses the same red/dark tone at times and it's difficult to see. 3. The screen flashes white when you get hit. It's really disorienting. 4. Sometimes you need to turn into chicken, which makes your character even smaller and thus even harder to see.  Compared to your usual metroidvanias, this one has a ton of platforming (just like Guacamelee 1). So if platforming is really not for you, skip it. I enjoyed the game while it lasted, but the bonus challenges are way too much for me.  I'm not gonna complain about the DLC since it's cosmetics and bonus challenges; the former, I don't care, the latter, I hate.  The bad parts of the game are optional, and yeah it's a great game.  The Good: 1. It's really easy to navigate. (Aside from the aforementioned challenges). I'm the kind of person who gets lost very easily, and having barrels littered around helps me see where I have/haven't gone before. I don't get lost in this game. 2. Very frequent checkpoints (aside from the aforementioned challenges, again). It really helps getting back in much quicker. 3. The combat is fun.  The Bad: 1. Head Statues are weirdly placed. Sometimes it places me smack dab in the middle of obstacles. I want to get to certain places, but it's far easier to just use the statue to teleport to somewhere adjacent to the area you want to travel, then go the portal there. 2. Instant death/spikes really make the game worse. Having to wait for the stage to load (even for a little bit) and not being able to get in action right away is really a bummer. 3. Aforementioned visual clarity, which is worse than Guacamelee 1. 4. Certain upgrades are very OP. 5. Instant-death enemies (the exploder) can be quite BS. But this is probably due to the lack of skills on my end as opposed to the design problems.  DrinkBox Studios really put their love and care in this one. Sometimes certain things aren't really my cup of tea, but I pushed through it and it was great. Final boss fight is very fun.
If you liked the first one you'll like this one, so I reccommend playing that one. The writing is a little flimsier, but the level design and combat is as good as ever.

很標準的類銀河戰士惡魔城 (metroidvania)  音樂聽起來真的蠻有墨西格風格 打擊感會隨著技能學到的程度越來越明顯 後期的戰鬥就會有每一招都充滿重量級傷害的感覺  戰鬥方面主要是以玩家首次走到一個區域就會進入類似競技場的狀態 入口出口關閉並且出現2~4波的敵人 全部敵人清除完畢以後再進入同樣的區域就不會要求強制戰鬥完才能離開 至於頭目戰個人覺得偏向簡單  非主線劇情的5項挑戰部分相當考驗操作 整體來說難度設計得不錯

This game is an improvement upon the original, no doubt.  The gameplay is way more intense, and it slims down the content experience to what is necessary this time around.  Upgrading is in the form of a skill tree this time.  I will say that I'm not a fan of you needing to earn all the abilities you had the first game, but then again I can see how this game would be boring if you had all the abilities from the beginning.  I will say though that sometimes the controls might not be as tight as I'd like them to be.  Sometimes I'd be pressing a button in order to sling shot and the game doesn't register my presses even though it's clear that it should've worked.  The challenge for platforming on the difficult sessions is even more difficult this time around.  In short, great game overall, and an improvement in pretty much every conceivable way over the predecessor

Vi pessoas reclamando do fato de que Guacamelee 2 parece mais uma dlc do que um jogo, por ser mais do mesmo, tais comentarios não estão 100% errados, o jogo é sim só uma extensão do primeiro, mas não vejo isso como um problema, aos que se apaixonaram por guacamelee 1, vão amar tanto quanto eu amei o 2, mas entendo que para algumas pessoas, essa falta de inovação drástica seja um problema. Se você comprou o primeiro e gostou, vá fundo no segundo, ele entrega um jogo divertido e ainda mais desafiador que o primeiro, tendo partes que levei dias para passar (prepare o rivotril antes de ir para "porta importante"). Em resumo, o jogo é maravilhoso e encantador igual o primeiro.

훌륭한 타격액션과 유머와 밈. B급정서 충만한 유쾌한 플랫포밍액션게임 과카몰리입니다.   하다보면 어려운 구간도 있지만, 계속 리젠되기 때문에 적당히 도전욕구를 자극하고  여러번 시도하다보면 분명 클리어하게 되는 적절한 플랫포밍 액션게임의 표본이라 할 수 있습니다.  미트보이 같은 게임에서 자신의 손꾸락을 탓하며 좌절감에 빠지셨다면 이 게임으로 다시 시작해보십시오.   당신에게도 컨트롤 피지컬이 남아있음을 알게 해줍니다.
有官中好评不停，游戏也很用心，画风，音乐，手感都能感觉到。它处买的key入的本体4.5元，目前就这，看以后追评。
